[2026-04-29T07:55:43.719Z] [STDOUT] {"body":"## Summary\n\nA single expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token (`DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) took down the entire release workflow on 2026-04-28 ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)): all **52** Docker build jobs failed at the identical `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, and the 14 downstream manifest/release jobs were skipped — blocking publication to **GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) was still perfectly valid.\n\nThis PR hardens `.github/workflows/release.yml` so a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) can no longer cascade into a full release blackout, and documents the rotation runbook so the on-call engineer knows what to do.\n\nFixes #82.\n\n## What changed\n\n- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences across `build-js-*`, `build-essentials-*`, `build-languages-*`, `build-dind-*`, `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push*`) now has `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, and is immediately followed by a `Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)` step that emits a `::warning` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook when the login fails. GHCR pushes proceed on their existing credentials when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- **`README.md`** — new `## Releasing` section documenting which secrets the release workflow expects (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) and a step-by-step Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`** — full incident write-up (timeline, root-cause analysis, contributing factor, alternatives considered, prevention).\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/ci-logs/`** — captured failed-step logs (728 lines) and run-level metadata for run `25073172386`.\n- **`experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`** — static-analysis sanity check that asserts all 15 login steps in `release.yml` are non-blocking and paired with a check step. Run with `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`.\n- **`.changeset/issue-82-tolerate-dockerhub-login-failure.md`** — patch-level changeset.\n\n## Reproducing\n\nThe bug is reproducible by:\n1. Generating a Docker Hub PAT with a short expiry (e.g. 1 day).\n2. Saving it as `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`.\n3. Waiting for it to expire.\n4. Triggering the workflow with `gh workflow run \"Build and Release Docker Image\" --ref main` (or a push to `main`).\n\nBefore this PR: every Docker build job fails at the login step; no images are published anywhere.\nAfter this PR: every Docker build job emits `::warning Docker Hub login failed` and continues; the GHCR portion of the release completes; the Docker Hub `docker push` calls inside `docker/build-push-action` and the existing retry-on-failure step fail loudly (so the run is still red), but GHCR is unblocked and the actionable rotation message appears in the run annotations.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `python3 -c \"import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))\"` — YAML still valid.\n- [x] `actionlint .github/workflows/release.yml` — no warnings.\n- [x] `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh` — passes (15 login steps, all non-blocking, all paired with a check step).\n- [x] PR-event CI on this branch passes (it does for run 25073270946 — `pull_request` events don't run the registry-pushing jobs, so the original failure mode does not show up here either way).\n- [ ] After merge to `main`: the next release run should succeed for GHCR even if `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` is still expired, and emit a clear warning annotation. This is the operational verification step.\n\n## Operational follow-up (cannot be done in code)\n\nPer the runbook now in `README.md` and `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`, a repo admin still needs to:\n\n1. Generate a fresh Docker Hub PAT with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes.\n2. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n3. Re-run [run 25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386) (or trigger `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`) to re-publish v2.1.0 to Docker Hub.","headRefName":"issue-82-9bbaad39cc07","headRepository":{"id":"R_kgDOQ2-R2Q","name":"box","nameWithOwner":"link-foundation/box"},"headRepositoryOwner":{"id":"O_kgDOCoAzvQ","login":"link-foundation"},"mergeStateStatus":"UNSTABLE","number":83,"state":"OPEN"}

Summary\n\nA single expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token (`DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) took down the entire release workflow on 2026-04-28 ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)): all **52** Docker build jobs failed at the identical `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, and the 14 downstream manifest/release jobs were skipped — blocking publication to **GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) was still perfectly valid.\n\nThis PR hardens `.github/workflows/release.yml` so a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) can no longer cascade into a full release blackout, and documents the rotation runbook so the on-call engineer knows what to do.\n\nFixes #82.\n\n## What changed\n\n- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences across `build-js-*`, `build-essentials-*`, `build-languages-*`, `build-dind-*`, `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push*`) now has `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, and is immediately followed by a `Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)` step that emits a `::warning` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook when the login fails. GHCR pushes proceed on their existing credentials when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- **`README.md`** — new `## Releasing` section documenting which secrets the release workflow expects (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) and a step-by-step Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`** — full incident write-up (timeline, root-cause analysis, contributing factor, alternatives considered, prevention).\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/ci-logs/`** — captured failed-step logs (728 lines) and run-level metadata for run `25073172386`.\n- **`experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`** — static-analysis sanity check that asserts all 15 login steps in `release.yml` are non-blocking and paired with a check step. Run with `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`.\n- **`.changeset/issue-82-tolerate-dockerhub-login-failure.md`** — patch-level changeset.\n\n## Reproducing\n\nThe bug is reproducible by:\n1. Generating a Docker Hub PAT with a short expiry (e.g. 1 day).\n2. Saving it as `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`.\n3. Waiting for it to expire.\n4. Triggering the workflow with `gh workflow run \"Build and Release Docker Image\" --ref main` (or a push to `main`).\n\nBefore this PR: every Docker build job fails at the login step; no images are published anywhere.\nAfter this PR: every Docker build job emits `::warning Docker Hub login failed` and continues; the GHCR portion of the release completes; the Docker Hub `docker push` calls inside `docker/build-push-action` and the existing retry-on-failure step fail loudly (so the run is still red), but GHCR is unblocked and the actionable rotation message appears in the run annotations.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `python3 -c \"import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))\"` — YAML still valid.\n- [x] `actionlint .github/workflows/release.yml` — no warnings.\n- [x] `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh` — passes (15 login steps, all non-blocking, all paired with a check step).\n- [x] PR-event CI on this branch passes (it does for run 25073270946 — `pull_request` events don't run the registry-pushing jobs, so the original failure mode does not show up here either way).\n- [ ] After merge to `main`: the next release run should succeed for GHCR even if `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` is still expired, and emit a clear warning annotation. This is the operational verification step.\n\n## Operational follow-up (cannot be done in code)\n\nPer the runbook now in `README.md` and `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`, a repo admin still needs to:\n\n1. Generate a fresh Docker Hub PAT with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes.\n2. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n3. Re-run [run 25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386) (or trigger `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`) to re-publish v2.1.0 to Docker [2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use the Read tool to view and analyze it. Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify that the file is a valid image rather than HTML by using a CLI tool such as the 'file' command. When corrupted or non-image files, such as GitHub \"Not Found\" pages saved as `.png`, are read, they can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. Instead: (1) when images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\", retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) when the retry still fails, skip the image and note that it was unavailable.

[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When encountering UI bugs, capture a screenshot showing the problem state, then create a visual regression test or manual verification screenshot after the fix.

[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When fetching lists from GitHub API, use the --paginate flag to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO] Playwright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When WebSearch tool fails or returns insufficient results, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for internet search.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use browser_take_screenshot from Playwright MCP.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When you finish using the browser, close it with browser_close to free resources.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When reproducing UI bugs, use browser_take_screenshot to capture the problem state before implementing any fix.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When fixing UI bugs, take before/after screenshots to provide visual evidence of the fix for human verification.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When creating UI tests, save baseline screenshots to the repository for visual regression testing.
[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When verifying UI fixes, compare screenshots to ensure the fix does not introduce unintended visual changes.

[2026-04-29T07:55:55.915Z] [INFO]    - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use the Read tool to view and analyze it. Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify that the file is a valid image rather than HTML by using a CLI tool such as the 'file' command. When corrupted or non-image files, such as GitHub "Not Found" pages saved as `.png`, are read, they can cause "Could not process image" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows "HTML", "text", or "ASCII text", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. Instead: (1) when images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing "github.com/user-attachments", retry with: curl -L -H "Authorization: token $(gh auth token)" -o <filename> "<url>" (2) when the retry still fails, skip the image and note that it was unavailable.

[2026-04-29T07:56:07.822Z] [INFO]         "content": "title:\tfix(ci): tolerate Docker Hub login failure (issue #82)\nstate:\tDRAFT\nauthor:\tkonard (Konstantin Diachenko)\nlabels:\t\nassignees:\tkonard (Konstantin Diachenko)\nreviewers:\t\nprojects:\t\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t83\nurl:\thttps://github.com/link-foundation/box/pull/83\nadditions:\t1137\ndeletions:\t0\nauto-merge:\tdisabled\n--\n## Summary\n\nA single expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token (`DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) took down the entire release workflow on 2026-04-28 ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)): all **52** Docker build jobs failed at the identical `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, and the 14 downstream manifest/release jobs were skipped — blocking publication to **GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) was still perfectly valid.\n\nThis PR hardens `.github/workflows/release.yml` so a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) can no longer cascade into a full release blackout, and documents the rotation runbook so the on-call engineer knows what to do.\n\nFixes #82.\n\n## What changed\n\n- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences across `build-js-*`, `build-essentials-*`, `build-languages-*`, `build-dind-*`, `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push*`) now has `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, and is immediately followed by a `Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)` step that emits a `::warning` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook when the login fails. GHCR pushes proceed on their existing credentials when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- **`README.md`** — new `## Releasing` section documenting which secrets the release workflow expects (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) and a step-by-step Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`** — full incident write-up (timeline, root-cause analysis, contributing factor, alternatives considered, prevention).\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/ci-logs/`** — captured failed-step logs (728 lines) and run-level metadata for run `25073172386`.\n- **`experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`** — static-analysis sanity check that asserts all 15 login steps in `release.yml` are non-blocking and paired with a check step. Run with `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`.\n- **`.changeset/issue-82-tolerate-dockerhub-login-failure.md`** — patch-level changeset.\n\n## Reproducing\n\nThe bug is reproducible by:\n1. Generating a Docker Hub PAT with a short expiry (e.g. 1 day).\n2. Saving it as `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`.\n3. Waiting for it to expire.\n4. Triggering the workflow with `gh workflow run \"Build and Release Docker Image\" --ref main` (or a push to `main`).\n\nBefore this PR: every Docker build job fails at the login step; no images are published anywhere.\nAfter this PR: every Docker build job emits `::warning Docker Hub login failed` and continues; the GHCR portion of the release completes; the Docker Hub `docker push` calls inside `docker/build-push-action` and the existing retry-on-failure step fail loudly (so the run is still red), but GHCR is unblocked and the actionable rotation message appears in the run annotations.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `python3 -c \"import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))\"` — YAML still valid.\n- [x] `actionlint .github/workflows/release.yml` — no warnings.\n- [x] `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh` — passes (15 login steps, all non-blocking, all paired with a check step).\n- [x] PR-event CI on this branch passes (it does for run 25073270946 — `pull_request` events don't run the registry-pushing jobs, so the original failure mode does not show up here either way).\n- [ ] After merge to `main`: the next release run should succeed for GHCR even if `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` is still expired, and emit a clear warning annotation. This is the operational verification step.\n\n## Operational follow-up (cannot be done in code)\n\nPer the runbook now in `README.md` and `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`, a repo admin still needs to:\n\n1. Generate a fresh Docker Hub PAT with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes.\n2. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n3. Re-run [run 25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386) (or trigger `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`) to re-publish v2.1.0 to Docker Hub.",

[2026-04-29T07:56:07.822Z] [INFO]     "stdout": "title:\tfix(ci): tolerate Docker Hub login failure (issue #82)\nstate:\tDRAFT\nauthor:\tkonard (Konstantin Diachenko)\nlabels:\t\nassignees:\tkonard (Konstantin Diachenko)\nreviewers:\t\nprojects:\t\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t83\nurl:\thttps://github.com/link-foundation/box/pull/83\nadditions:\t1137\ndeletions:\t0\nauto-merge:\tdisabled\n--\n## Summary\n\nA single expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token (`DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) took down the entire release workflow on 2026-04-28 ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)): all **52** Docker build jobs failed at the identical `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, and the 14 downstream manifest/release jobs were skipped — blocking publication to **GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) was still perfectly valid.\n\nThis PR hardens `.github/workflows/release.yml` so a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) can no longer cascade into a full release blackout, and documents the rotation runbook so the on-call engineer knows what to do.\n\nFixes #82.\n\n## What changed\n\n- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences across `build-js-*`, `build-essentials-*`, `build-languages-*`, `build-dind-*`, `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push*`) now has `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, and is immediately followed by a `Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)` step that emits a `::warning` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook when the login fails. GHCR pushes proceed on their existing credentials when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- **`README.md`** — new `## Releasing` section documenting which secrets the release workflow expects (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) and a step-by-step Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`** — full incident write-up (timeline, root-cause analysis, contributing factor, alternatives considered, prevention).\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/ci-logs/`** — captured failed-step logs (728 lines) and run-level metadata for run `25073172386`.\n- **`experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`** — static-analysis sanity check that asserts all 15 login steps in `release.yml` are non-blocking and paired with a check step. Run with `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`.\n- **`.changeset/issue-82-tolerate-dockerhub-login-failure.md`** — patch-level changeset.\n\n## Reproducing\n\nThe bug is reproducible by:\n1. Generating a Docker Hub PAT with a short expiry (e.g. 1 day).\n2. Saving it as `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`.\n3. Waiting for it to expire.\n4. Triggering the workflow with `gh workflow run \"Build and Release Docker Image\" --ref main` (or a push to `main`).\n\nBefore this PR: every Docker build job fails at the login step; no images are published anywhere.\nAfter this PR: every Docker build job emits `::warning Docker Hub login failed` and continues; the GHCR portion of the release completes; the Docker Hub `docker push` calls inside `docker/build-push-action` and the existing retry-on-failure step fail loudly (so the run is still red), but GHCR is unblocked and the actionable rotation message appears in the run annotations.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `python3 -c \"import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))\"` — YAML still valid.\n- [x] `actionlint .github/workflows/release.yml` — no warnings.\n- [x] `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh` — passes (15 login steps, all non-blocking, all paired with a check step).\n- [x] PR-event CI on this branch passes (it does for run 25073270946 — `pull_request` events don't run the registry-pushing jobs, so the original failure mode does not show up here either way).\n- [ ] After merge to `main`: the next release run should succeed for GHCR even if `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` is still expired, and emit a clear warning annotation. This is the operational verification step.\n\n## Operational follow-up (cannot be done in code)\n\nPer the runbook now in `README.md` and `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`, a repo admin still needs to:\n\n1. Generate a fresh Docker Hub PAT with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes.\n2. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n3. Re-run [run 25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386) (or trigger `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`) to re-publish v2.1.0 to Docker Hub.",

Each base image now has a sibling `<image>-dind` that ships the Docker Engine, CLI, containerd, Buildx, and Compose v2 plus an entrypoint that starts the inner `dockerd` and drops to the `box` user: - `konard/box` → `konard/box-dind` - `konard/box-essentials` → `konard/box-essentials-dind` - `konard/box-js` → `konard/box-js-dind` - `konard/box-<language>-dind` for every language box (python, go, rust, java, kotlin, ruby, php, perl, swift, lean, rocq) All variants are multi-arch (linux/amd64 + linux/arm64) on Docker Hub and ghcr.io. Recommended invocation: `docker run --runtime=sysbox-runc konard/box-dind` (Sysbox, no `--privileged` needed). Default fallback: `docker run --privileged konard/box-dind`. Each running dind-box has its own inner Docker daemon, so `docker ps -a` from inside the container only lists containers created by that container — the host-safety stretch goal in issue #80. echo \"$CHANGED_FILES\" | grep -qE '^ubuntu/24\\.04/dind/'; then\n            echo \"dind=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"dind=false\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n      - name: Detect per-language changes\n        id: language-changes\n        run: |\n          CHANGED_FILES=$(cat /tmp/changed-files.txt)\n\n          for lang in python go rust java kotlin ruby php perl swift lean rocq cpp assembly; do\n            if echo \"$CHANGED_FILES\" | grep -qE \"^ubuntu/24\\.04/${lang}/\"; then\n              echo \"${lang}=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            else\n              echo \"${lang}=false\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            fi\n          done\n\n      - name: Determine if build is needed\n        id: should-build\n        run: |\n          # For workflow_dispatch, always build (regardless of mode)\n          if [ \"${{ github.event_name }}\" = \"workflow_dispatch\" ]; then\n            echo \"result=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            echo \"Build triggered by: manual dispatch (mode: ${{ github.event.inputs.release_mode }})\"\n            exit 0\n          fi\n\n          # Trigger build on any relevant changes\n          if [ \"${{ steps.changes.outputs.docker }}\" = \"true\" ]; then\n            echo \"result=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            echo \"Build triggered by: Dockerfile changes\"\n          elif [ \"${{ steps.changes.outputs.scripts }}\" = \"true\" ]; then\n            echo \"result=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            echo \"Build triggered by: scripts changes\"\n          elif [ \"${{ steps.changes.outputs.ubuntu }}\" = \"true\" ]; then\n            echo \"result=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            echo \"Build triggered by: ubuntu modular scripts changes\"\n          elif [ \"${{ steps.changes.outputs.workflow }}\" = \"true\" ]; then\n            echo \"result=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            echo \"Build triggered by: workflow changes\"\n          elif [ \"${{ steps.changes.outputs.version }}\" = \"true\" ]; then\n            echo \"result=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            echo \"Build triggered by: VERSION file changes\"\n          else\n            echo \"result=false\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            echo \"No build needed: no relevant changes detected\"\n          fi\n\n  # === BUILD AND TEST DOCKER IMAGE (PR) ===\n  docker-build-test:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04\n    needs: [detect-changes, version-check, changeset-check]\n    # Use always() to prevent implicit success() check from skipping this job\n    # when upstream jobs are skipped (see docs/case-studies/issue-23)\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      needs.detect-changes.result == 'success' &&\n      (needs.version-check.result == 'success' || needs.version-check.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') &&\n      github.event_name == 'pull_request' &&\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.should-build == 'true'\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3\n\n      - name: Build layered images (JS -> essentials -> languages -> full)\n        run: |\n          echo \"=== Building JS box ===\"\n          docker build -f ubuntu/24.04/js/Dockerfile -t box-js .\n\n          echo \"\"\n          echo \"=== Building essentials box (on JS) ===\"\n          docker build -f ubuntu/24.04/essentials-box/Dockerfile \\\n            --build-arg JS_IMAGE=box-js -t box-essentials .\n\n          echo \"\"\n          echo \"=== Building language images (on essentials) ===\"\n          for lang in python go rust java kotlin ruby php perl swift lean rocq; do\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"--- Building ${lang} box ---\"\n            docker build -f \"ubuntu/24.04/${lang}/Dockerfile\" \\\n              --build-arg ESSENTIALS_IMAGE=box-essentials \\\n              -t \"box-${lang}\" .\n          done\n\n          echo \"\"\n          echo \"=== Building full box (multi-stage from all language images) ===\"\n          docker build -f ubuntu/24.04/full-box/Dockerfile \\\n            --build-arg ESSENTIALS_IMAGE=box-essentials \\\n            --build-arg PYTHON_IMAGE=box-python \\\n            --build-arg GO_IMAGE=box-go \\\n            --build-arg RUST_IMAGE=box-rust \\\n            --build-arg JAVA_IMAGE=box-java \\\n            --build-arg KOTLIN_IMAGE=box-kotlin \\\n            --build-arg RUBY_IMAGE=box-ruby \\\n            --build-arg PHP_IMAGE=box-php \\\n            --build-arg PERL_IMAGE=box-perl \\\n            --build-arg SWIFT_IMAGE=box-swift \\\n            --build-arg LEAN_IMAGE=box-lean \\\n            --build-arg ROCQ_IMAGE=box-rocq \\\n            -t box-test .\n\n      - name: Test JS box\n        run: |\n          set -e\n          echo \"=== Testing JS box ===\"\n          docker run --rm box-js bash -c '. $HOME/.nvm/nvm.sh && node --version'\n          docker run --rm box-js bash -c 'export PATH=$HOME/.bun/bin:$PATH && bun --version'\n          docker run --rm box-js bash -c 'export PATH=$HOME/.deno/bin:$PATH && deno --version'\n          echo \"=== JS box tests passed ===\"\n\n      - name: Test essentials box\n        run: |\n          set -e\n          echo \"=== Testing essentials box ===\"\n          docker run --rm box-essentials gh --version\n          docker run --rm box-essentials glab --version\n          docker run --rm box-essentials gh-setup-git-identity --version\n          docker run --rm box-essentials glab-setup-git-identity --version\n          echo \"=== Essentials box tests passed ===\"\n\n      - name: Test full box\n        run: |\n          set -e\n          echo \"=== Testing full box ===\"\n          echo \"Note: Using entrypoint script which initializes all environments\"\n\n          # JavaScript/TypeScript runtimes\n          docker run --rm box-test node --version\n          docker run --rm box-test bun --version\n          docker run --rm box-test deno --version\n\n          # Python (pyenv)\n          docker run --rm box-test python3 --version\n          docker run --rm box-test pip3 --version\n\n          # Go\n          docker run --rm box-test go version\n\n          # Rust (rustup + cargo + \\\n            --build-arg BASE_IMAGE=box-test \\\n            -t box-dind-test .\n\n      - name: Test dind-box variant\n        run: |\n          set -e\n          echo \"=== Testing dind-box (CLI box is the base layer - built first, other images depend on it\n  build-js-amd64:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      needs.detect-changes.result == 'success' &&\n      (\n        (github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.should-build == 'true') ||\n        (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')\n      ) &&\n      (\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.js-changed == 'true' ||\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.common-changed == 'true' ||\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.version-changed == 'true' ||\n        github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'\n      )\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n    outputs:\n      built: ${{ steps.result.outputs.built }}\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          ref: main\n\n      - name: Get latest version\n        id: version\n        run: |\n          git pull origin main || true\n          VERSION=$(cat VERSION | tr -d '[:space:]')\n          echo \"version=$VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3\n\n      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ github.actor }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      # Issue #82: tolerate Docker Hub login failure (e.g. expired DOCKERHUB_TOKEN)\n      # so that pushes to GHCR still proceed instead of taking down every build job.\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        id: dockerhub-login\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)\n        if: steps.dockerhub-login.outcome != 'success'\n        run: |\n          echo \"::warning title=Docker Hub login failed::Docker Hub login failed (outcome=${{ steps.dockerhub-login.outcome }}). The DOCKERHUB_TOKEN repository secret is most likely expired or revoked. See docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md for the rotation runbook. The job will continue and push to GHCR; pushes to docker.io will be skipped or fail.\"\n\n      - name: Build and push JS box (amd64)\n        id: build-push\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ubuntu/24.04/js/Dockerfile\n          platforms: linux/amd64\n          push: true\n          tags: |\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:latest-amd64\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:latest-amd64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64\n          provenance: false\n          cache-from: type=gha,scope=js-amd64\n          cache-to: type=gha,scope=js-amd64,mode=max\n\n      # Retry push on transient GHCR 403 errors (Issue #78)\n      - name: Retry push JS box (amd64) on failure\n        if: steps.build-push.outcome == 'failure'\n        run: |\n          echo \"First push attempt failed, retrying with backoff...\"\n          for attempt in 1 2 3; do\n            echo \"==> Retry attempt $attempt/3...\"\n            if docker buildx build \\\n              --file ubuntu/24.04/js/Dockerfile \\\n              --platform linux/amd64 \\\n              --tag ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:latest-amd64 \\\n              --tag ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64 \\\n              --tag ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:latest-amd64 \\\n              --tag ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64 \\\n              --provenance=false \\\n              --cache-from type=gha,scope=js-amd64 \\\n              --push \\\n              .; then\n              echo \"==> Push succeeded on retry attempt $attempt\"\n              exit 0\n            fi\n            if [ \"$attempt\" -lt 3 ]; then\n              delay=$((10 * attempt))\n              echo \"==> Retry failed, waiting ${delay}s before next attempt...\"\n              sleep \"$delay\"\n            fi\n          done\n          echo \"==> All retry attempts failed\"\n          exit 1\n\n      - name: Mark as built\n        id: result\n        run: echo \"built=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n  # === BUILD JS BOX (arm64) ===\n  build-js-arm64:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04-arm\n    timeout-minutes: 120\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      needs.detect-changes.result == 'success' &&\n      (\n        (github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.should-build == 'true') ||\n        (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')\n      ) &&\n      (\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.js-changed == 'true' ||\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.common-changed == 'true' ||\n        needs.detect-changes.outputs.version-changed == 'true' ||\n        github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'\n      )\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n    outputs:\n      built: ${{ steps.result.outputs.built }}\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3\n\n      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ github.actor }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      # Issue #82: tolerate Docker Hub login failure (e.g. expired DOCKERHUB_TOKEN)\n      # so that pushes to GHCR still proceed instead of taking down every build job.\n      - The job will continue and push to GHCR; pushes to docker.io will be skipped or fail.\"\n\n      - name: Build and push JS box (arm64)\n        id: build-push\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ubuntu/24.04/js/Dockerfile\n          platforms: linux/arm64\n          push: true\n          tags: |\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:latest-arm64\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-arm64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:latest-arm64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-arm64\n          provenance: false\n          cache-from: type=gha,scope=js-arm64\n          cache-to: type=gha,scope=js-arm64,mode=max\n\n      # Retry push on transient GHCR 403 errors (Issue #78)\n      - The DOCKERHUB_TOKEN repository secret is most likely expired or revoked. See docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md for the rotation runbook. The job will continue and push to GHCR; pushes to docker.io will be skipped or fail.\"\n\n      - name: Determine JS base image\n        id: js-base\n        run: |\n          # Use freshly built JS image if JS was rebuilt, otherwise use latest\n          if [ \"${{ needs.build-js-amd64.outputs.built }}\" = \"true\" ]; then\n            echo \"image=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"image=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-js:latest-amd64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n      - name: Build and push essentials box (amd64)\n        id: build-push\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ubuntu/24.04/essentials-box/Dockerfile\n          platforms: linux/amd64\n          push: true\n          build-args: |\n            JS_IMAGE=${{ steps.js-base.outputs.image }}\n          tags: |\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:latest-amd64\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:latest-amd64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64\n          provenance: false\n          cache-from: type=gha,scope=essentials-amd64\n          cache-to: type=gha,scope=essentials-amd64,mode=max\n\n      # Retry push on transient GHCR 403 errors (Issue #78)\n      - name: Retry push essentials box (amd64) on failure\n        if: steps.build-push.outcome == 'failure'\n        run: |\n          echo \"First push attempt failed, retrying with backoff...\"\n          for attempt in 1 2 3; do\n            echo \"==> Retry attempt $attempt/3...\"\n            if docker buildx build \\\n              --file ubuntu/24.04/essentials-box/Dockerfile \\\n              --platform linux/amd64 \\\n              --build-arg JS_IMAGE=${{ steps.js-base.outputs.image }} \\\n              --tag ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:latest-amd64 \\\n              --tag ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64 \\\n              --tag ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:latest-amd64 \\\n              --tag ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64 \\\n              --provenance=false \\\n              --cache-from type=gha,scope=essentials-amd64 \\\n              --push \\\n              .; then\n              echo \"==> Push succeeded on retry attempt $attempt\"\n              exit 0\n            fi\n            if [ \"$attempt\" -lt 3 ]; then\n              delay=$((10 * attempt))\n              echo \"==> Retry failed, waiting ${delay}s before next attempt...\"\n              sleep \"$delay\"\n            fi\n          done\n          echo \"==> All retry attempts failed\"\n          exit 1\n\n      - password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)\n        if: steps.check-lang.outputs.should_build == 'true' && steps.dockerhub-login.outcome != 'success'\n        run: |\n          echo \"::warning title=Docker Hub login failed::Docker Hub login failed (outcome=${{ steps.dockerhub-login.outcome }}). The DOCKERHUB_TOKEN repository secret is most likely expired or revoked. See docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md for the rotation runbook. The job will continue and push to GHCR; pushes to docker.io will be skipped or fail.\"\n\n      - name: Determine essentials base image\n        if: steps.check-lang.outputs.should_build == 'true'\n        id: essentials-base\n        run: |\n          if [ \"${{ needs.build-essentials-arm64.outputs.built }}\" = \"true\" ]; then\n            echo \"image=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-arm64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"image=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:latest-arm64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.language }} box (arm64)\n        id: build-push\n        continue-on-error: true\n        if: steps.check-lang.outputs.should_build == 'true'\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ubuntu/24.04/${{ matrix.language }}/Dockerfile\n          platforms: linux/arm64\n          push: true\n          build-args: |\n            ESSENTIALS_IMAGE=${{ steps.essentials-base.outputs.image }}\n          tags: |\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${{ matrix.language }}:latest-arm64\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${{ matrix.language }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-arm64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${{ matrix.language }}:latest-arm64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${{ matrix.language }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-arm64\n          provenance: false\n          cache-from: type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.language }}-arm64\n          cache-to: type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.language }}-arm64,mode=max\n\n      # Retry push on transient GHCR 403 errors (Issue #78)\n      - name: Retry push ${{ matrix.language }} box (arm64) on failure\n        if: steps.check-lang.outputs.should_build == 'true' && steps.build-push.outcome == 'failure'\n        run: |\n          echo \"First push attempt failed, retrying with backoff...\"\n          TAGS=(\n            \"${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${{ matrix.language }}:latest-arm64\"\n            \"${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${{ matrix.language }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-arm64\"\n            \"${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${{ matrix.language }}:latest-arm64\"\n            \"${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${{ matrix.language }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-arm64\"\n          )\n          TAG_ARGS=\"\"\n          for tag in \"${TAGS[@]}\"; do\n            TAG_ARGS=\"$TAG_ARGS --tag $tag\"\n          done\n          for attempt in 1 2 3; do\n            echo \"==> Retry attempt $attempt/3...\"\n            if docker buildx build \\\n              --file ubuntu/24.04/${{ matrix.language }}/Dockerfile \\\n              --platform linux/arm64 \\\n              --build-arg ESSENTIALS_IMAGE=${{ steps.essentials-base.outputs.image }} \\\n              $TAG_ARGS \\\n              --provenance=false \\\n              --cache-from type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.language }}-arm64 \\\n              --push \\\n              .; then\n              echo \"==> Push succeeded on retry attempt $attempt\"\n              exit 0\n            fi\n            if [ \"$attempt\" -lt 3 ]; then\n              delay=$((10 * attempt))\n              echo \"==> Retry failed, waiting ${delay}s before next attempt...\"\n              sleep \"$delay\"\n            fi\n          done\n          echo \"==> All retry attempts failed\"\n          exit 1\n\n      # PHP-specific: Add local/global suffix tags based on install method (Issue #44)\n      - name: Tag PHP image with install method suffix (arm64)\n        if: steps.check-lang.outputs.should_build == 'true' && matrix.language == 'php'\n        run: |\n          VERSION=\"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"\n          # Pull the image and inspect the marker file\n          docker pull ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64\n          PHP_METHOD=$(docker run --rm ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64 cat /home/box/.php-install-method 2>/dev/null || echo \"unknown\")\n          echo \"PHP install method (arm64): $PHP_METHOD\"\n\n          if [ \"$PHP_METHOD\" = \"local\" ] || [ \"$PHP_METHOD\" = \"global\" ]; then\n            # Tag with method suffix on both registries\n            docker tag ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64 \\\n              ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:latest-arm64-${PHP_METHOD}\n            docker tag ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64 \\\n              ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64-${PHP_METHOD}\n            docker tag ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64 \\\n              ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:latest-arm64-${PHP_METHOD}\n            docker tag ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64 \\\n              ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64-${PHP_METHOD}\n\n            docker push ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:latest-arm64-${PHP_METHOD}\n            docker push ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64-${PHP_METHOD}\n            docker push ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:latest-arm64-${PHP_METHOD}\n            docker push ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-php:${VERSION}-arm64-${PHP_METHOD}\n\n            echo \"Tagged PHP image with -${PHP_METHOD} suffix\"\n          fi\n\n  # === CREATE LANGUAGE MULTI-ARCH MANIFESTS ===\n  languages-manifest:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04\n    needs: [detect-changes, build-languages-amd64, build-languages-arm64]\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        language: [python, go, rust, java, kotlin, ruby, php, perl, swift, lean, rocq]\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      needs.detect-changes.result == 'success' &&\n      needs.build-languages-amd64.result == 'success' &&\n      needs.build-languages-arm64.result == 'success'\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          ref: main\n\n      - name: Get latest version\n        id: version\n        run: |\n          git pull origin main || true\n          VERSION=$(cat VERSION | tr -d '[:space:]')\n          echo \"version=$VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ github.actor }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      # Issue #82: tolerate Docker Hub login failure (e.g. expired DOCKERHUB_TOKEN)\n      # so that pushes to GHCR still proceed instead of taking down every build job.\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        id: dockerhub-login\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)\n        if: steps.dockerhub-login.outcome != 'success'\n        run: |\n          echo \"::warning title=Docker Hub login failed::Docker Hub login failed (outcome=${{ steps.dockerhub-login.outcome }}). The DOCKERHUB_TOKEN repository secret is most likely expired or revoked. See docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md for the rotation runbook. The job will continue and push to GHCR; pushes to docker.io will be skipped or fail.\"\n\n      - name: Create and push ${{ matrix.language }} multi-arch manifests\n        run: |\n          VERSION=\"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"\n          LANG=\"${{ matrix.language }}\"\n\n          # GHCR\n          docker manifest create ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:latest \\\n            --amend ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:latest-amd64 \\\n            --amend ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:latest-arm64\n          docker manifest push ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:latest\n\n          docker manifest create ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:${VERSION} \\\n            --amend ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:${VERSION}-amd64 \\\n            --amend ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:${VERSION}-arm64\n          docker manifest push ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:${VERSION}\n\n          # Docker Hub\n          docker manifest create ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:latest \\\n            --amend ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:latest-amd64 \\\n            --amend ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:latest-arm64\n          docker manifest push ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:latest\n\n          docker manifest create ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:${VERSION} \\\n            --amend ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:${VERSION}-amd64 \\\n            --amend ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:${VERSION}-arm64\n          docker manifest push ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${LANG}:${VERSION}\n\n          echo \"${LANG} box multi-arch manifests pushed for latest and ${VERSION}\"\n\n  # === BUILD AND PUSH FULL BOX (MAIN - amd64) ===\n  docker-build-push:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04\n    needs: [detect-changes, build-languages-amd64, build-essentials-amd64]\n    # Run on push to main with changes, OR on workflow_dispatch\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      needs.detect-changes.result == 'success' &&\n      (needs.build-essentials-amd64.result == 'success' || needs.build-essentials-amd64.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.build-languages-amd64.result == 'success' || needs.build-languages-amd64.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (\n        (github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.should-build == 'true') ||\n        (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')\n      )\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    outputs:\n      version: ${{ needs.detect-changes.outputs.version }}\n      digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          ref: main  # Always use latest main for releases\n\n      # Fix for issue #41: Free disk space to prevent \"No space left on device\" errors\n      # This step removes unnecessary pre-installed software from the runner to free ~30 GB\n      # See: docs/case-studies/issue-41/CASE-STUDY.md\n      - name: Free disk space\n        uses: jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main\n        with:\n          tool-cache: false  # Keep tool cache for setup-* action compatibility\n          android: true      # Free ~14 GB\n          dotnet: true       # Free ~2.7 GB\n          haskell: true      # Free ~0 GB (not pre-installed on ubuntu-24.04)\n          large-packages: true  # Free ~5.3 GB\n          docker-images: true   # Clean existing Docker images\n          swap-storage: true    # Free ~4 GB\n\n      - name: Get latest version\n        id: version\n        run: |\n          git pull origin main || true\n          VERSION=$(cat VERSION | tr -d '[:space:]')\n          echo \"version=$VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"Building version: $VERSION\"\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3\n\n      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ github.actor }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      # Issue #82: tolerate Docker Hub login failure (e.g. expired DOCKERHUB_TOKEN)\n      # so that pushes to GHCR still proceed instead of taking down every build job.\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        id: dockerhub-login\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)\n        if: steps.dockerhub-login.outcome != 'success'\n        run: |\n          echo \"::warning title=Docker Hub login failed::Docker Hub login failed (outcome=${{ steps.dockerhub-login.outcome }}). The DOCKERHUB_TOKEN repository secret is most likely expired or revoked. See docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md for the rotation runbook. The job will continue and push to GHCR; pushes to docker.io will be skipped or fail.\"\n\n      - name: Determine base images\n        id: base-images\n        run: |\n          VERSION=\"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"\n\n          # Essentials base image\n          if [ \"${{ needs.build-essentials-amd64.outputs.built }}\" = \"true\" ]; then\n            echo \"essentials=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:${VERSION}-amd64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"essentials=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-essentials:latest-amd64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n          # Language images - use version tag if languages were built, otherwise latest\n          for lang in python go rust java kotlin ruby php perl swift lean rocq; do\n            LANG_UPPER=$(echo \"$lang\" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')\n            # If the language matrix ran successfully, use versioned tag\n            if [ \"${{ needs.build-languages-amd64.result }}\" = \"success\" ]; then\n              echo \"${lang}=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${lang}:${VERSION}-amd64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            else\n              echo \"${lang}=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${lang}:latest-amd64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            fi\n          done\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5\n        with:\n          images: |\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}\n          tags: |\n            type=raw,value=latest\n            type=raw,value=${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\n            type=sha,prefix=\n            type=raw,value={{date 'YYYYMMDD'}}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata for amd64-specific tags\n        id: meta-amd64\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5\n        with:\n          images: |\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}\n          flavor: |\n            suffix=-amd64\n          tags: |\n            type=raw,value=latest\n            type=raw,value=${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\n            type=sha,prefix=\n            type=raw,value={{date 'YYYYMMDD'}}\n\n      - name: Build and push full box (amd64)\n        id: build\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ubuntu/24.04/full-box/Dockerfile\n          platforms: linux/amd64\n          push: true\n          build-args: |\n            ESSENTIALS_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.essentials }}\n            PYTHON_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.python }}\n            GO_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.go }}\n            RUST_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.rust }}\n            JAVA_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.java }}\n            KOTLIN_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.kotlin }}\n            RUBY_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.ruby }}\n            PHP_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.php }}\n            PERL_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.perl }}\n            SWIFT_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.swift }}\n            LEAN_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.lean }}\n            ROCQ_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.rocq }}\n          tags: |\n            ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\n            ${{ steps.meta-amd64.outputs.tags }}\n          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}\n          provenance: false  # Prevents unknown/unknown platform in registry\n          cache-from: type=gha\n          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max\n\n      # Retry push on transient GHCR 403 errors (Issue #78)\n      - name: Retry push full box (amd64) on failure\n        if: steps.build.outcome == 'failure'\n        env:\n          ALL_TAGS: |\n            ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\n            ${{ steps.meta-amd64.outputs.tags }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"First push attempt failed, retrying with backoff...\"\n          TAG_ARGS=\"\"\n          while IFS= read -r tag; do\n            tag=$(echo \"$tag\" | xargs)\n            [ -z \"$tag\" ] && continue\n            TAG_ARGS=\"$TAG_ARGS --tag $tag\"\n          done <<< \"$ALL_TAGS\"\n          for attempt in 1 2 3; do\n            echo \"==> Retry attempt $attempt/3...\"\n            if docker buildx build \\\n              --file ubuntu/24.04/full-box/Dockerfile \\\n              --platform linux/amd64 \\\n              --build-arg ESSENTIALS_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.essentials }} \\\n              --build-arg PYTHON_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.python }} \\\n              --build-arg GO_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.go }} \\\n              --build-arg RUST_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.rust }} \\\n              --build-arg JAVA_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.java }} \\\n              --build-arg KOTLIN_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.kotlin }} \\\n              --build-arg RUBY_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.ruby }} \\\n              --build-arg PHP_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.php }} \\\n              --build-arg PERL_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.perl }} \\\n              --build-arg SWIFT_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.swift }} \\\n              --build-arg LEAN_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.lean }} \\\n              --build-arg ROCQ_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.rocq }} \\\n              $TAG_ARGS \\\n              --provenance=false \\\n              --cache-from type=gha \\\n              --push \\\n              .; then\n              echo \"==> Push succeeded on retry attempt $attempt\"\n              exit 0\n            fi\n            if [ \"$attempt\" -lt 3 ]; then\n              delay=$((10 * attempt))\n              echo \"==> Retry failed, waiting ${delay}s before next attempt...\"\n              sleep \"$delay\"\n            fi\n          done\n          echo \"==> All retry attempts failed\"\n          exit 1\n\n      # Smoke test the published image to verify all toolchains work (issue #62)\n      # Tests each toolchain command to catch cases where tools are missing/broken\n      - name: Smoke test released full box (amd64)\n        run: |\n          set -e\n          VERSION=\"${{          fi\n\n          # Language images\n          for lang in python go rust java kotlin ruby php perl swift lean rocq; do\n            if [ \"${{ needs.build-languages-arm64.result }}\" = \"success\" ]; then\n              echo \"${lang}=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${lang}:${VERSION}-arm64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            else\n              echo \"${lang}=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}-${lang}:latest-arm64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n            fi\n          done\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5\n        with:\n          images: |\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}\n          flavor: |\n            suffix=-arm64\n          tags: |\n            type=raw,value=latest\n            type=raw,value=${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\n            type=sha,prefix=\n            type=raw,value={{date 'YYYYMMDD'}}\n\n      - name: Build and push full box (arm64)\n        id: build\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ubuntu/24.04/full-box/Dockerfile\n          platforms: linux/arm64\n          push: true\n          build-args: |\n            ESSENTIALS_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.essentials }}\n            PYTHON_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.python }}\n            GO_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.go }}\n            RUST_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.rust }}\n            JAVA_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.java }}\n            KOTLIN_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.kotlin }}\n            RUBY_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.ruby }}\n            PHP_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.php }}\n            PERL_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.perl }}\n            SWIFT_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.swift }}\n            LEAN_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.lean }}\n            ROCQ_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.rocq }}\n          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\n          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}\n          provenance: false  # Prevents unknown/unknown platform in registry\n          cache-from: type=gha\n          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max\n\n      # Retry push on transient GHCR 403 errors (Issue #78)\n      - name: Retry push full box (arm64) on failure\n        if: steps.build.outcome == 'failure'\n        env:\n          ALL_TAGS: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"First push attempt failed, retrying with backoff...\"\n          TAG_ARGS=\"\"\n          while IFS= read -r tag; do\n            tag=$(echo \"$tag\" | xargs)\n            [ -z \"$tag\" ] && continue\n            TAG_ARGS=\"$TAG_ARGS --tag $tag\"\n          done <<< \"$ALL_TAGS\"\n          for attempt in 1 2 3; do\n            echo \"==> Retry attempt $attempt/3...\"\n            if docker buildx build \\\n              --file ubuntu/24.04/full-box/Dockerfile \\\n              --platform linux/arm64 \\\n              --build-arg ESSENTIALS_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.essentials }} \\\n              --build-arg PYTHON_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.python }} \\\n              --build-arg GO_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.go }} \\\n              --build-arg RUST_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.rust }} \\\n              --build-arg JAVA_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.java }} \\\n              --build-arg KOTLIN_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.kotlin }} \\\n              --build-arg RUBY_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.ruby }} \\\n              --build-arg PHP_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.php }} \\\n              --build-arg PERL_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.perl }} \\\n              --build-arg SWIFT_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.swift }} \\\n              --build-arg LEAN_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.lean }} \\\n              --build-arg ROCQ_IMAGE=${{ steps.base-images.outputs.rocq }} \\\n              $TAG_ARGS \\\n              --provenance=false \\\n              --cache-from type=gha \\\n              --push \\\n              .; then\n              echo \"==> Push succeeded on retry attempt $attempt\"\n              exit 0\n            fi\n            if [ \"$attempt\" -lt 3 ]; then\n              delay=$((10 * attempt))\n              echo \"==> Retry failed, waiting ${delay}s before next attempt...\"\n              sleep \"$delay\"\n            fi\n          done\n          echo \"==> All retry attempts failed\"\n          exit 1\n\n  # === CREATE MULTI-ARCH MANIFEST ===\n  docker-manifest:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04\n    needs: [detect-changes, docker-build-push, docker-build-push-arm64]\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      needs.detect-changes.result == 'success' &&\n      needs.docker-build-push.result == 'success' &&\n      needs.docker-build-push-arm64.result == 'success' &&\n      (\n        (github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main') ||\n        (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')\n      )\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          ref: main\n\n      - name: Get latest version\n        id: version\n        run: |\n          git pull origin main || true\n          VERSION=$(cat VERSION | tr -d '[:space:]')\n          echo \"version=$VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"Creating manifest for version: $VERSION\"\n\n      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ github.actor }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      # Issue #82: tolerate Docker Hub login failure (e.g. expired DOCKERHUB_TOKEN)\n      # so that pushes to GHCR still proceed instead of taking down every build job.\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        id: dockerhub-login\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)\n        if: steps.dockerhub-login.outcome != 'success'\n        run: |\n          echo \"::warning title=Docker Hub login failed::Docker Hub login failed (outcome=${{ steps.dockerhub-login.outcome }}). The DOCKERHUB_TOKEN repository secret is most likely expired or revoked. See docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md for the rotation runbook. The job will continue and push to GHCR; pushes to docker.io will be skipped or fail.\"\n\n      - name: Create and push multi-arch manifest (GHCR)\n        run: |\n          VERSION=\"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"\n\n          # Create manifest for latest tag on GHCR\n          docker manifest create ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}:latest \\\n            --amend ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}:latest-amd64 \\\n            --amend ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}:latest-arm64\n\n          docker manifest push ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}:latest\n\n          # Create manifest for version tag on GHCR\n          docker manifest create ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}:${VERSION} \\\n            --amend ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}:${VERSION}-amd64 \\\n            --amend ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}:${VERSION}-arm64\n\n          docker manifest push ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}:${VERSION}\n\n          echo \"GHCR multi-arch manifest created and pushed successfully for latest and ${VERSION}\"\n\n      - name: Create and push multi-arch manifest (Docker Hub)\n        run: |\n          VERSION=\"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"\n\n          # Create manifest for latest tag on Docker Hub\n          docker manifest create ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}:latest \\\n            --amend ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}:latest-amd64 \\\n            --amend ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}:latest-arm64\n\n          docker manifest push ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}:latest\n\n          # Create manifest for version tag on Docker Hub\n          docker manifest create ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}:${VERSION} \\\n            --amend ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}:${VERSION}-amd64 \\\n            --amend ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}:${VERSION}-arm64\n\n          docker manifest push ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}:${VERSION}\n\n          echo \"Docker Hub multi-arch manifest created and pushed successfully for latest and ${VERSION}\"\n\n  # === BUILD DIND-BOX VARIANTS (amd64) - issue #80 ===\n  # Layers Docker Engine on top of every base box image so each variant has a\n  # \"<base>-dind\" sibling. Runs after the source variant's manifest is published.\n  build-dind-amd64:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04\n    timeout-minutes: 30\n    needs: [detect-changes, js-manifest, essentials-manifest, languages-manifest, docker-manifest]\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        # variant -> base box flavour. Special value \"full\" maps to konard/box.\n        # Everything else maps to konard/box-<variant>.\n        variant: [js, essentials, python, go, rust, java, kotlin, ruby, php, perl, swift, lean, rocq, full]\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      needs.detect-changes.result == 'success' &&\n      (needs.js-manifest.result == 'success' || needs.js-manifest.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.essentials-manifest.result == 'success' || needs.essentials-manifest.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.languages-manifest.result == 'success' || needs.languages-manifest.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.docker-manifest.result == 'success' || needs.docker-manifest.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (\n        (github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.should-build == 'true') ||\n        (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')\n      )\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          ref: main\n\n      - name: Get latest version\n        id: version\n        run: |\n          git pull origin main || true\n          VERSION=$(cat VERSION | tr -d '[:space:]')\n          echo \"version=$VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n      - name: Compute image names\n        id: names\n        run: |\n          NAME=\"${{ matrix.variant }}\"\n          # Source base image: full -> \"konard/box\"; others -> \"konard/box-<name>\"\n          # Target dind image: full -> \"konard/box-dind\"; others -> \"konard/box-<name>-dind\"\n          if [ \"$NAME\" = \"full\" ]; then\n            BASE_SUFFIX=\"\"\n            DIND_SUFFIX=\"-dind\"\n          else\n            BASE_SUFFIX=\"-$NAME\"\n            DIND_SUFFIX=\"-${NAME}-dind\"\n          fi\n          echo \"base_image=${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}${BASE_SUFFIX}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"dind_suffix=${DIND_SUFFIX}\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3\n\n      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ github.actor }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      # Issue #82: tolerate Docker Hub login failure (e.g. expired DOCKERHUB_TOKEN)\n      # so that pushes to GHCR still proceed instead of taking down every build job.\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        id: dockerhub-login\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/login-action@v3\n        with:\n          registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)\n        if: steps.dockerhub-login.outcome != 'success'\n        run: |\n          echo \"::warning title=Docker Hub login failed::Docker Hub login failed (outcome=${{ steps.dockerhub-login.outcome }}). The DOCKERHUB_TOKEN repository secret is most likely expired or revoked. See docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md for the rotation runbook. The job will continue and push to GHCR; pushes to docker.io will be skipped or fail.\"\n\n      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.variant }} dind-box (amd64)\n        id: build-push\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ubuntu/24.04/dind/Dockerfile\n          platforms: linux/amd64\n          push: true\n          build-args: |\n            BASE_IMAGE=${{ steps.names.outputs.base_image }}\n          tags: |\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}${{ steps.names.outputs.dind_suffix }}:latest-amd64\n            ${{ env.GHCR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.GHCR_IMAGE_NAME }}${{ steps.names.outputs.dind_suffix }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}${{ steps.names.outputs.dind_suffix }}:latest-amd64\n            ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME }}${{ steps.names.outputs.dind_suffix }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-amd64\n          provenance: false\n          cache-from: type=gha,scope=dind-${{ matrix.variant }}-amd64\n          cache-to: type=gha,scope=dind-${{ matrix.variant }}-amd64,mode=max\n\n      - The release was not produced and no images were published to either registry.\n13\t\n14\t## Timeline of Events\n15\t\n16\t| Time (UTC) | Event |\n17\t|---|---|\n18\t| 2026-04-28 13:26-14:28 | Issue #80 PR (#81) iterates: several pushes to `issue-80-bded956c66f7` initially fail (workflow file syntax issues), then succeed once the workflow is fixed. |\n19\t| 2026-04-28 14:28:48 | PR #81 final CI run on the branch passes (run `25058867725`). |\n20\t| 2026-04-28 ~19:25 | PR #81 is merged into `main` as commit `da0c1f3` (`2.1.0: Add dind-box family`). |\n21\t| 2026-04-28 19:26:28 | Push to `main` triggers `Build and Release Docker Image` workflow (run `25073172386`). |\n22\t| 2026-04-28 19:27:00 | First failure: `build-js-amd64` → `Log in to Docker Hub` fails with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`. |\n23\t| 2026-04-28 19:27:08 → 19:28:29 | Within ~80 seconds, **all 52 build jobs** fail at the same login step with the same expired-token error — across `js`, `essentials` (skipped via dependency), all 11 `languages-*` matrix jobs (×amd64/arm64), and all 14 `dind-*` matrix jobs (×amd64/arm64). |\n24\t| 2026-04-28 19:28:29 | Last build job fails. Downstream `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push`, `create-release` jobs are skipped because their `needs:` upstream did not succeed. |\n25\t| 2026-04-28 19:28:41 | Issue #82 is opened, linking the failing run. |\n26\t| 2026-04-28 19:28:48 | This PR's branch `issue-82-9bbaad39cc07` opens a PR run (#83) which **passes**, because the `pull_request` event in `release.yml` only runs `version-check`, `changeset-check`, and `detect-changes` (no registry pushes). |\n27\t\n28\t## Failed Jobs\n29\t\n30\tAll 52 failures share the same root cause. Jobs (run-id `25073172386`):\n31\t\n32\t| Group | Architectures | Count |\n33\t|---|---|---|\n34\t| `build-js-{amd64,arm64}` | both | 2 |\n35\t| `build-languages-{amd64,arm64}` × 11 languages | both | 22 |\n36\t| `build-dind-{amd64,arm64}` × 14 variants | both | 28 |\n37\t| **Total** | | **52** |\n38\t\n39\t(`build-essentials-*` were skipped because the `js` upstream failed; the language and dind matrices have weaker dependencies and ran independently — all of them then died on the same login step.)\n40\t\n41\tSkipped because of the broken dependency chain (14): `Check for Manual Version Changes`, `version-bump`, `Check for Changesets`, `docker-build-test`, `build-essentials-amd64`, `js-manifest`, `build-essentials-arm64`, `essentials-manifest`, `docker-build-push`, `languages-manifest`, `docker-build-push-arm64`, `docker-manifest`, `dind-manifest`, `create-release`.\n42\t\n43\tFull failed-step logs are saved at [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) (728 lines). A run summary is at [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json).\n44\t\n45\t## Root Cause Analysis\n46\t\n47\t### Primary root cause: expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token\n48\t\n49\tThe `secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` repository secret used by every `docker/login-action@v3` step in `.github/workflows/release.yml` is a Docker Hub Personal Access Token (PAT). PATs on Docker Hub have an immutable expiration date — once expired, they cannot be renewed; a new token must be generated and the secret rotated. From [Docker's docs](https://docs.docker.com/security/for-developers/access-tokens/):\n50\t\n51\t> You cannot modify the expiration date after a token is created. You must create a new PAT if you need to set a new expiration date.\n52\t\n53\tWhen the token expires, every `docker/login-action` call to `docker.io` returns:\n54\t\n55\t```\n56\tError response from daemon: Get \"https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/\":\n57\tunauthorized: personal access token is expired\n58\t```\n59\t\n60\tThis is a 30-day-default token rotation policy issue, not a code bug per se — but the workflow **amplifies** the impact into a complete release outage.\n61\t\n62\t### Contributing factor: the workflow has no graceful degradation\n63\t\n64\tThe release workflow logs into both **GHCR** (via the auto-provisioned `GITHUB_TOKEN`) and **Docker Hub** (via `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`). The Docker Hub login step is identical in every build job:\n65\t\n66\t```yaml\n67\t- name: Log in to Docker Hub\n68\t  uses: docker/login-action@v3\n69\t  with:\n70\t    registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n71\t    username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n72\t    password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n73\t```\n74\t\n75\tThere is no `continue-on-error: true`, no fallback, and no conditional pruning of Docker Hub tags from the subsequent `docker/build-push-action` step. As a result, an expired Docker Hub token takes down **every push to GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential is still perfectly valid.\n76\t\n77\tThis is the exact same anti-pattern that issue #78 highlighted (no retry on transient GHCR 403s) — a single transient/external failure cascades into a workflow-wide outage.\n78\t\n79\t### Why the PR (#83) check still passes\n80\t"
