[2026-04-29T07:57:45.553Z] [INFO]       "content": "# Case Study: Issue #82 - CI/CD Pipeline Hard-Fails on Expired Docker Hub Token\n\n## Summary\n\nThe CI/CD release workflow ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)) failed on **2026-04-28 at 19:26 UTC** when the `da0c1f3` merge commit was pushed to `main` (PR #81 merge commit for the `dind-box` family from issue #80). **Every** Docker build job failed at the same step — `Log in to Docker Hub` — with the identical error:\n\n```\n##[error]Error response from daemon: Get \"https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/\":\nunauthorized: personal access token is expired\n```\n\nOut of 68 jobs in the run, **52 failed, 14 were skipped (because their `needs:` chain was broken), and only 2 succeeded** (`Apply Changesets`, `detect-changes`). The release was not produced and no images were published to either registry.\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Time (UTC) | Event |\n|---|---|\n| 2026-04-28 13:26-14:28 | Issue #80 PR (#81) iterates: several pushes to `issue-80-bded956c66f7` initially fail (workflow file syntax issues), then succeed once the workflow is fixed. |\n| 2026-04-28 14:28:48 | PR #81 final CI run on the branch passes (run `25058867725`). |\n| 2026-04-28 ~19:25 | PR #81 is merged into `main` as commit `da0c1f3` (`2.1.0: Add dind-box family`). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:26:28 | Push to `main` triggers `Build and Release Docker Image` workflow (run `25073172386`). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:27:00 | First failure: `build-js-amd64` → `Log in to Docker Hub` fails with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:27:08 → 19:28:29 | Within ~80 seconds, **all 52 build jobs** fail at the same login step with the same expired-token error — across `js`, `essentials` (skipped via dependency), all 11 `languages-*` matrix jobs (×amd64/arm64), and all 14 `dind-*` matrix jobs (×amd64/arm64). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:29 | Last build job fails. Downstream `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push`, `create-release` jobs are skipped because their `needs:` upstream did not succeed. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:41 | Issue #82 is opened, linking the failing run. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:48 | This PR's branch `issue-82-9bbaad39cc07` opens a PR run (#83) which **passes**, because the `pull_request` event in `release.yml` only runs `version-check`, `changeset-check`, and `detect-changes` (no registry pushes). |\n\n## Failed Jobs\n\nAll 52 failures share the same root cause. Jobs (run-id `25073172386`):\n\n| Group | Architectures | Count |\n|---|---|---|\n| `build-js-{amd64,arm64}` | both | 2 |\n| `build-languages-{amd64,arm64}` × 11 languages | both | 22 |\n| `build-dind-{amd64,arm64}` × 14 variants | both | 28 |\n| **Total** | | **52** |\n\n(`build-essentials-*` were skipped because the `js` upstream failed; the language and dind matrices have weaker dependencies and ran independently — all of them then died on the same login step.)\n\nSkipped because of the broken dependency chain (14): `Check for Manual Version Changes`, `version-bump`, `Check for Changesets`, `docker-build-test`, `build-essentials-amd64`, `js-manifest`, `build-essentials-arm64`, `essentials-manifest`, `docker-build-push`, `languages-manifest`, `docker-build-push-arm64`, `docker-manifest`, `dind-manifest`, `create-release`.\n\nFull failed-step logs are saved at [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) (728 lines). A run summary is at [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json).\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary root cause: expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token\n\nThe `secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` repository secret used by every `docker/login-action@v3` step in `.github/workflows/release.yml` is a Docker Hub Personal Access Token (PAT). PATs on Docker Hub have an immutable expiration date — once expired, they cannot be renewed; a new token must be generated and the secret rotated. From [Docker's docs](https://docs.docker.com/security/for-developers/access-tokens/):\n\n> You cannot modify the expiration date after a token is created. You must create a new PAT if you need to set a new expiration date.\n\nWhen the token expires, every `docker/login-action` call to `docker.io` returns:\n\n```\nError response from daemon: Get \"https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/\":\nunauthorized: personal access token is expired\n```\n\nThis is a 30-day-default token rotation policy issue, not a code bug per se — but the workflow **amplifies** the impact into a complete release outage.\n\n### Contributing factor: the workflow has no graceful degradation\n\nThe release workflow logs into both **GHCR** (via the auto-provisioned `GITHUB_TOKEN`) and **Docker Hub** (via `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`). The Docker Hub login step is identical in every build job:\n\n```yaml\n- name: Log in to Docker Hub\n  uses: docker/login-action@v3\n  with:\n    registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n    username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n    password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n```\n\nThere is no `continue-on-error: true`, no fallback, and no conditional pruning of Docker Hub tags from the subsequent `docker/build-push-action` step. As a result, an expired Docker Hub token takes down **every push to GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential is still perfectly valid.\n\nThis is the exact same anti-pattern that issue #78 highlighted (no retry on transient GHCR 403s) — a single transient/external failure cascades into a workflow-wide outage.\n\n### Why the PR (#83) check still passes\n",

[2026-04-29T07:57:48.668Z] [INFO]         "content": "80\t\n81\t`release.yml` only runs `Log in to Docker Hub` for `push`/`workflow_dispatch` events. On `pull_request` events, only the lightweight `version-check`, `changeset-check`, and `detect-changes` jobs run, so the expired-token failure is invisible until the PR is merged into `main`.\n82\t\n83\t## Requirements (from the issue body)\n84\t\n85\t1. Download all logs/data and compile them under `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` — **done** (this folder).\n86\t2. Reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events — **done** (above).\n87\t3. List all requirements from the issue — **this section**.\n88\t4. Find the root cause(s) of each problem — **done** (single root cause with one contributing factor).\n89\t5. Propose solutions and a solution plan — **see below**.\n90\t6. Check known existing components/libraries for similar problems — **see \"Existing solutions\" below**.\n91\t7. Add debug output / verbose mode if root cause is unclear — **N/A**, root cause is unambiguous from the logs.\n92\t8. If the issue is in another project, file an upstream issue — **N/A**, this is a configuration/workflow issue local to this repo.\n93\t\n94\t## Solution Plan\n95\t\n96\tThe fix is two-track: an immediate operational rotation of the Docker Hub PAT (cannot be done in code — requires a repo admin), plus workflow hardening so that a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) does not cause a full release blackout again.\n97\t\n98\t### Track A — Operational (manual, one-time)\n99\t\n100\tA repo admin must:\n101\t\n102\t1. Sign in to Docker Hub at https://hub.docker.com/settings/security\n103\t2. Generate a new Personal Access Token with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes for the `konard/box*` repositories. Set an expiry that fits the project's rotation policy (e.g. 90 days), and add a calendar reminder to rotate.\n104\t3. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n105\t4. Re-run the failed `Build and Release Docker Image` workflow (`gh run rerun 25073172386 --repo link-foundation/box`) — or push an empty commit / use `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`.\n106\t\n107\tThis step is documented in [`README.md` → \"Releasing\"](../../../README.md) as part of this PR.\n108\t\n109\t### Track B — Workflow hardening (this PR)\n110\t\n111\tMake the release workflow degrade gracefully when Docker Hub is unavailable, so that:\n112\t\n113\t* **GHCR keeps publishing** even when Docker Hub login fails.\n114\t* The job exits **non-zero** at the end if Docker Hub was the intended target, so the failure is still visible — but only after GHCR has been pushed.\n115\t* The error message in the GHA log clearly tells the next engineer \"rotate `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`\".\n116\t\n117\tThe minimal, low-risk implementation:\n118\t\n119\t1. Add `continue-on-error: true` to every `Log in to Docker Hub` step and capture the step's `outcome` via an `id`.\n120\t2. Add a small `Check Docker Hub login` step right after that emits a clear, actionable warning when the login failed (matching the expired-token error specifically) and exposes a `dockerhub-available` step output.\n121\t3. Leave the existing `docker/build-push-action` push to both registries in place. When Docker Hub is down, those pushes will fail and the existing **retry logic** (added in issue #78) will kick in. The retry logic already wraps each `docker/build-push-action` and retries with `docker buildx build --push`, which respects whatever credentials have been logged in. If Docker Hub is unreachable, the GHCR tags will still succeed, and the retry block will then fail on the Docker Hub tags only — but at that point GHCR has the image and the retry log makes it clear which registry blocked the job.\n122\t\n123\tThe smallest change that fixes the catastrophic-cascade behavior without restructuring the entire workflow is:\n124\t\n125\t* `continue-on-error: true` on every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences in `release.yml`).\n126\t* A new \"Check Docker Hub login\" step right after that prints a very loud, actionable error message when the login failed, naming the secret to rotate. This is what the next engineer will see in the GHA UI, and it's how we avoid a future debugging dead-end.\n127\t\n128\tThis change is intentionally narrow:\n129\t\n130\t* It does **not** restructure the build matrix.\n131\t* It does **not** drop Docker Hub from the publish targets — when the token is rotated, behavior is unchanged.\n132\t* It does **not** swap the cross-job base-image source from Docker Hub to GHCR (a desirable follow-up but bigger surgery, deferred).\n133\t\n134\t### Track C — Documentation (this PR)\n135\t\n136\tAdd a \"Releasing\" section to the project `README.md` and to `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md` (this file) that:\n137\t\n138\t* Documents which secrets the release workflow needs (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`).\n139\t* Names Docker Hub PAT expiry as a known operational hazard.\n140\t* Gives the rotation runbook step-by-step.\n141\t\n142\t## Existing Solutions Considered\n143\t\n144\t| Approach | Verdict |\n145\t|---|---|\n146\t| **Use OIDC federation to Docker Hub** instead of a long-lived PAT | Docker Hub does **not** support OIDC trust as of writing (only paid orgs have org-token support; PATs remain the recommended GHA path). [docker/login-action#229](https://github.com/docker/login-action/issues/229) tracks this. Not actionable today. |\n147\t| **Use a Docker Hub *organization* access token** (admin-managed, rotatable) | Available on paid Docker Team/Business; same expiry semantics. Would still need rotation but is centrally managed. Out of scope for this PR. |\n148\t| **Drop Docker Hub entirely, publish only to GHCR** | Project's release notes (`create-release` job) and `README.md` advertise both registries; users may have pulled `konard/box*:latest` directly from Docker Hub. Removing it is a breaking change and not requested by the issue. |\n149\t| **Monitor PAT expiry with a scheduled job** that pings Docker Hub every day and opens an issue if auth fails | Reasonable next step. Tracked as a follow-up — the `schedule:` block can be added to `release.yml` or split out, paired with a `peter-evans/create-issue-from-file` action. Not in this PR. |\n150\t| **Make `Log in to Docker Hub` non-blocking** (this PR) | Smallest blast radius, immediately stops a token expiry from cascading into a 52-job failure. |\n151\t\n152\t## Prevention\n153\t\n154\t* `continue-on-error: true` on `Log in to Docker Hub` everywhere ensures that a future token expiry only fails the Docker Hub *push* attempts, not the upstream login itself — so GHCR pushes complete and the release pipeline still produces images that users can pull.\n155\t* The new \"Check Docker Hub login\" step's loud error message reduces mean-time-to-diagnosis from \"investigate 52 failing jobs\" to \"read one annotation in the run summary\".\n156\t* The README rotation runbook removes the \"what do I do?\" step from the on-call response.\n157\t\n158\t## Files\n159\t\n160\t* [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) — full failed-step logs, all 52 jobs.\n161\t* [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json) — run-level metadata.\n162\t"

[2026-04-29T07:57:48.668Z] [INFO]       "content": "\n`release.yml` only runs `Log in to Docker Hub` for `push`/`workflow_dispatch` events. On `pull_request` events, only the lightweight `version-check`, `changeset-check`, and `detect-changes` jobs run, so the expired-token failure is invisible until the PR is merged into `main`.\n\n## Requirements (from the issue body)\n\n1. Download all logs/data and compile them under `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` — **done** (this folder).\n2. Reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events — **done** (above).\n3. List all requirements from the issue — **this section**.\n4. Find the root cause(s) of each problem — **done** (single root cause with one contributing factor).\n5. Propose solutions and a solution plan — **see below**.\n6. Check known existing components/libraries for similar problems — **see \"Existing solutions\" below**.\n7. Add debug output / verbose mode if root cause is unclear — **N/A**, root cause is unambiguous from the logs.\n8. If the issue is in another project, file an upstream issue — **N/A**, this is a configuration/workflow issue local to this repo.\n\n## Solution Plan\n\nThe fix is two-track: an immediate operational rotation of the Docker Hub PAT (cannot be done in code — requires a repo admin), plus workflow hardening so that a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) does not cause a full release blackout again.\n\n### Track A — Operational (manual, one-time)\n\nA repo admin must:\n\n1. Sign in to Docker Hub at https://hub.docker.com/settings/security\n2. Generate a new Personal Access Token with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes for the `konard/box*` repositories. Set an expiry that fits the project's rotation policy (e.g. 90 days), and add a calendar reminder to rotate.\n3. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n4. Re-run the failed `Build and Release Docker Image` workflow (`gh run rerun 25073172386 --repo link-foundation/box`) — or push an empty commit / use `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`.\n\nThis step is documented in [`README.md` → \"Releasing\"](../../../README.md) as part of this PR.\n\n### Track B — Workflow hardening (this PR)\n\nMake the release workflow degrade gracefully when Docker Hub is unavailable, so that:\n\n* **GHCR keeps publishing** even when Docker Hub login fails.\n* The job exits **non-zero** at the end if Docker Hub was the intended target, so the failure is still visible — but only after GHCR has been pushed.\n* The error message in the GHA log clearly tells the next engineer \"rotate `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`\".\n\nThe minimal, low-risk implementation:\n\n1. Add `continue-on-error: true` to every `Log in to Docker Hub` step and capture the step's `outcome` via an `id`.\n2. Add a small `Check Docker Hub login` step right after that emits a clear, actionable warning when the login failed (matching the expired-token error specifically) and exposes a `dockerhub-available` step output.\n3. Leave the existing `docker/build-push-action` push to both registries in place. When Docker Hub is down, those pushes will fail and the existing **retry logic** (added in issue #78) will kick in. The retry logic already wraps each `docker/build-push-action` and retries with `docker buildx build --push`, which respects whatever credentials have been logged in. If Docker Hub is unreachable, the GHCR tags will still succeed, and the retry block will then fail on the Docker Hub tags only — but at that point GHCR has the image and the retry log makes it clear which registry blocked the job.\n\nThe smallest change that fixes the catastrophic-cascade behavior without restructuring the entire workflow is:\n\n* `continue-on-error: true` on every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences in `release.yml`).\n* A new \"Check Docker Hub login\" step right after that prints a very loud, actionable error message when the login failed, naming the secret to rotate. This is what the next engineer will see in the GHA UI, and it's how we avoid a future debugging dead-end.\n\nThis change is intentionally narrow:\n\n* It does **not** restructure the build matrix.\n* It does **not** drop Docker Hub from the publish targets — when the token is rotated, behavior is unchanged.\n* It does **not** swap the cross-job base-image source from Docker Hub to GHCR (a desirable follow-up but bigger surgery, deferred).\n\n### Track C — Documentation (this PR)\n\nAdd a \"Releasing\" section to the project `README.md` and to `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md` (this file) that:\n\n* Documents which secrets the release workflow needs (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`).\n* Names Docker Hub PAT expiry as a known operational hazard.\n* Gives the rotation runbook step-by-step.\n\n## Existing Solutions Considered\n\n| Approach | Verdict |\n|---|---|\n| **Use OIDC federation to Docker Hub** instead of a long-lived PAT | Docker Hub does **not** support OIDC trust as of writing (only paid orgs have org-token support; PATs remain the recommended GHA path). [docker/login-action#229](https://github.com/docker/login-action/issues/229) tracks this. Not actionable today. |\n| **Use a Docker Hub *organization* access token** (admin-managed, rotatable) | Available on paid Docker Team/Business; same expiry semantics. Would still need rotation but is centrally managed. Out of scope for this PR. |\n| **Drop Docker Hub entirely, publish only to GHCR** | Project's release notes (`create-release` job) and `README.md` advertise both registries; users may have pulled `konard/box*:latest` directly from Docker Hub. Removing it is a breaking change and not requested by the issue. |\n| **Monitor PAT expiry with a scheduled job** that pings Docker Hub every day and opens an issue if auth fails | Reasonable next step. Tracked as a follow-up — the `schedule:` block can be added to `release.yml` or split out, paired with a `peter-evans/create-issue-from-file` action. Not in this PR. |\n| **Make `Log in to Docker Hub` non-blocking** (this PR) | Smallest blast radius, immediately stops a token expiry from cascading into a 52-job failure. |\n\n## Prevention\n\n* `continue-on-error: true` on `Log in to Docker Hub` everywhere ensures that a future token expiry only fails the Docker Hub *push* attempts, not the upstream login itself — so GHCR pushes complete and the release pipeline still produces images that users can pull.\n* The new \"Check Docker Hub login\" step's loud error message reduces mean-time-to-diagnosis from \"investigate 52 failing jobs\" to \"read one annotation in the run summary\".\n* The README rotation runbook removes the \"what do I do?\" step from the on-call response.\n\n## Files\n\n* [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) — full failed-step logs, all 52 jobs.\n* [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json) — run-level metadata.\n",

[2026-04-29T07:58:14.659Z] [INFO]           "new_string": "## Follow-up: second CI/CD bug surfaced by this PR's own CI\n\nAfter the initial fix in this PR (PAT-tolerance), the next PR-event run [`25075335426`](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25075335426) failed in the `docker-build-test` job with a different error:\n\n```\n#37 ERROR: failed to copy files: copy file range failed: no space left on device\n##[warning]You are running out of disk space. The runner will stop working when the machine runs out of disk space. Free space left: 0 MB\n```\n\nThe failure occurs at `Dockerfile:99` (`full-box`) on `COPY --from=ruby-stage --chown=box:box /home/box/.rbenv /home/box/.rbenv`, after the job has already built **JS → essentials → 11 language images → full-box** sequentially on a single `ubuntu-24.04` runner.\n\n### Root cause\n\n`docker-build-test` (PR-only smoke test, lines 403-564 of `release.yml`) does not free disk space before the build. The publish-jobs (`docker-build-push`, `docker-build-push-arm64`) already use `jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main` to reclaim ~30 GB before building (added in [issue #41](../../issue-41/CASE-STUDY.md)), but the PR-CI smoke test was missed. As more language images were added (most recently `dind` family in #80), cumulative disk usage from BuildKit's working set tipped over the runner's default ~22 GB free.\n\n### Fix in this PR\n\nAdd the same `jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main` step to `docker-build-test`, with the same `with:` block already used in `docker-build-push`:\n\n```yaml\n- name: Free disk space\n  uses: jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main\n  with:\n    tool-cache: false\n    android: true\n    dotnet: true\n    haskell: true\n    large-packages: true\n    docker-images: true\n    swap-storage: true\n```\n\nCaptured logs: [`ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log`](./ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log).\n\n## Files\n\n* [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) — full failed-step logs from the original `release.yml` PAT-expiry incident, all 52 jobs.\n* [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json) — run-level metadata for the original incident.\n* [`ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log`](./ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log) — log capture of the follow-up `docker-build-test` disk-exhaustion failure on PR run `25075335426`."

[2026-04-29T07:58:14.676Z] [INFO]     "newString": "## Follow-up: second CI/CD bug surfaced by this PR's own CI\n\nAfter the initial fix in this PR (PAT-tolerance), the next PR-event run [`25075335426`](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25075335426) failed in the `docker-build-test` job with a different error:\n\n```\n#37 ERROR: failed to copy files: copy file range failed: no space left on device\n##[warning]You are running out of disk space. The runner will stop working when the machine runs out of disk space. Free space left: 0 MB\n```\n\nThe failure occurs at `Dockerfile:99` (`full-box`) on `COPY --from=ruby-stage --chown=box:box /home/box/.rbenv /home/box/.rbenv`, after the job has already built **JS → essentials → 11 language images → full-box** sequentially on a single `ubuntu-24.04` runner.\n\n### Root cause\n\n`docker-build-test` (PR-only smoke test, lines 403-564 of `release.yml`) does not free disk space before the build. The publish-jobs (`docker-build-push`, `docker-build-push-arm64`) already use `jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main` to reclaim ~30 GB before building (added in [issue #41](../../issue-41/CASE-STUDY.md)), but the PR-CI smoke test was missed. As more language images were added (most recently `dind` family in #80), cumulative disk usage from BuildKit's working set tipped over the runner's default ~22 GB free.\n\n### Fix in this PR\n\nAdd the same `jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main` step to `docker-build-test`, with the same `with:` block already used in `docker-build-push`:\n\n```yaml\n- name: Free disk space\n  uses: jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main\n  with:\n    tool-cache: false\n    android: true\n    dotnet: true\n    haskell: true\n    large-packages: true\n    docker-images: true\n    swap-storage: true\n```\n\nCaptured logs: [`ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log`](./ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log).\n\n## Files\n\n* [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) — full failed-step logs from the original `release.yml` PAT-expiry incident, all 52 jobs.\n* [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json) — run-level metadata for the original incident.\n* [`ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log`](./ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log) — log capture of the follow-up `docker-build-test` disk-exhaustion failure on PR run `25075335426`.",
[2026-04-29T07:58:14.676Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "# Case Study: Issue #82 - CI/CD Pipeline Hard-Fails on Expired Docker Hub Token\n\n## Summary\n\nThe CI/CD release workflow ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)) failed on **2026-04-28 at 19:26 UTC** when the `da0c1f3` merge commit was pushed to `main` (PR #81 merge commit for the `dind-box` family from issue #80). **Every** Docker build job failed at the same step — `Log in to Docker Hub` — with the identical error:\n\n```\n##[error]Error response from daemon: Get \"https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/\":\nunauthorized: personal access token is expired\n```\n\nOut of 68 jobs in the run, **52 failed, 14 were skipped (because their `needs:` chain was broken), and only 2 succeeded** (`Apply Changesets`, `detect-changes`). The release was not produced and no images were published to either registry.\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Time (UTC) | Event |\n|---|---|\n| 2026-04-28 13:26-14:28 | Issue #80 PR (#81) iterates: several pushes to `issue-80-bded956c66f7` initially fail (workflow file syntax issues), then succeed once the workflow is fixed. |\n| 2026-04-28 14:28:48 | PR #81 final CI run on the branch passes (run `25058867725`). |\n| 2026-04-28 ~19:25 | PR #81 is merged into `main` as commit `da0c1f3` (`2.1.0: Add dind-box family`). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:26:28 | Push to `main` triggers `Build and Release Docker Image` workflow (run `25073172386`). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:27:00 | First failure: `build-js-amd64` → `Log in to Docker Hub` fails with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:27:08 → 19:28:29 | Within ~80 seconds, **all 52 build jobs** fail at the same login step with the same expired-token error — across `js`, `essentials` (skipped via dependency), all 11 `languages-*` matrix jobs (×amd64/arm64), and all 14 `dind-*` matrix jobs (×amd64/arm64). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:29 | Last build job fails. Downstream `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push`, `create-release` jobs are skipped because their `needs:` upstream did not succeed. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:41 | Issue #82 is opened, linking the failing run. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:48 | This PR's branch `issue-82-9bbaad39cc07` opens a PR run (#83) which **passes**, because the `pull_request` event in `release.yml` only runs `version-check`, `changeset-check`, and `detect-changes` (no registry pushes). |\n\n## Failed Jobs\n\nAll 52 failures share the same root cause. Jobs (run-id `25073172386`):\n\n| Group | Architectures | Count |\n|---|---|---|\n| `build-js-{amd64,arm64}` | both | 2 |\n| `build-languages-{amd64,arm64}` × 11 languages | both | 22 |\n| `build-dind-{amd64,arm64}` × 14 variants | both | 28 |\n| **Total** | | **52** |\n\n(`build-essentials-*` were skipped because the `js` upstream failed; the language and dind matrices have weaker dependencies and ran independently — all of them then died on the same login step.)\n\nSkipped because of the broken dependency chain (14): `Check for Manual Version Changes`, `version-bump`, `Check for Changesets`, `docker-build-test`, `build-essentials-amd64`, `js-manifest`, `build-essentials-arm64`, `essentials-manifest`, `docker-build-push`, `languages-manifest`, `docker-build-push-arm64`, `docker-manifest`, `dind-manifest`, `create-release`.\n\nFull failed-step logs are saved at [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) (728 lines). A run summary is at [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json).\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary root cause: expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token\n\nThe `secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` repository secret used by every `docker/login-action@v3` step in `.github/workflows/release.yml` is a Docker Hub Personal Access Token (PAT). PATs on Docker Hub have an immutable expiration date — once expired, they cannot be renewed; a new token must be generated and the secret rotated. From [Docker's docs](https://docs.docker.com/security/for-developers/access-tokens/):\n\n> You cannot modify the expiration date after a token is created. You must create a new PAT if you need to set a new expiration date.\n\nWhen the token expires, every `docker/login-action` call to `docker.io` returns:\n\n```\nError response from daemon: Get \"https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/\":\nunauthorized: personal access token is expired\n```\n\nThis is a 30-day-default token rotation policy issue, not a code bug per se — but the workflow **amplifies** the impact into a complete release outage.\n\n### Contributing factor: the workflow has no graceful degradation\n\nThe release workflow logs into both **GHCR** (via the auto-provisioned `GITHUB_TOKEN`) and **Docker Hub** (via `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`). The Docker Hub login step is identical in every build job:\n\n```yaml\n- name: Log in to Docker Hub\n  uses: docker/login-action@v3\n  with:\n    registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n    username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n    password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n```\n\nThere is no `continue-on-error: true`, no fallback, and no conditional pruning of Docker Hub tags from the subsequent `docker/build-push-action` step. As a result, an expired Docker Hub token takes down **every push to GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential is still perfectly valid.\n\nThis is the exact same anti-pattern that issue #78 highlighted (no retry on transient GHCR 403s) — a single transient/external failure cascades into a workflow-wide outage.\n\n### Why the PR (#83) check still passes\n\n`release.yml` only runs `Log in to Docker Hub` for `push`/`workflow_dispatch` events. On `pull_request` events, only the lightweight `version-check`, `changeset-check`, and `detect-changes` jobs run, so the expired-token failure is invisible until the PR is merged into `main`.\n\n## Requirements (from the issue body)\n\n1. Download all logs/data and compile them under `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` — **done** (this folder).\n2. Reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events — **done** (above).\n3. List all requirements from the issue — **this section**.\n4. Find the root cause(s) of each problem — **done** (single root cause with one contributing factor).\n5. Propose solutions and a solution plan — **see below**.\n6. Check known existing components/libraries for similar problems — **see \"Existing solutions\" below**.\n7. Add debug output / verbose mode if root cause is unclear — **N/A**, root cause is unambiguous from the logs.\n8. If the issue is in another project, file an upstream issue — **N/A**, this is a configuration/workflow issue local to this repo.\n\n## Solution Plan\n\nThe fix is two-track: an immediate operational rotation of the Docker Hub PAT (cannot be done in code — requires a repo admin), plus workflow hardening so that a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) does not cause a full release blackout again.\n\n### Track A — Operational (manual, one-time)\n\nA repo admin must:\n\n1. Sign in to Docker Hub at https://hub.docker.com/settings/security\n2. Generate a new Personal Access Token with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes for the `konard/box*` repositories. Set an expiry that fits the project's rotation policy (e.g. 90 days), and add a calendar reminder to rotate.\n3. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n4. Re-run the failed `Build and Release Docker Image` workflow (`gh run rerun 25073172386 --repo link-foundation/box`) — or push an empty commit / use `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`.\n\nThis step is documented in [`README.md` → \"Releasing\"](../../../README.md) as part of this PR.\n\n### Track B — Workflow hardening (this PR)\n\nMake the release workflow degrade gracefully when Docker Hub is unavailable, so that:\n\n* **GHCR keeps publishing** even when Docker Hub login fails.\n* The job exits **non-zero** at the end if Docker Hub was the intended target, so the failure is still visible — but only after GHCR has been pushed.\n* The error message in the GHA log clearly tells the next engineer \"rotate `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`\".\n\nThe minimal, low-risk implementation:\n\n1. Add `continue-on-error: true` to every `Log in to Docker Hub` step and capture the step's `outcome` via an `id`.\n2. Add a small `Check Docker Hub login` step right after that emits a clear, actionable warning when the login failed (matching the expired-token error specifically) and exposes a `dockerhub-available` step output.\n3. Leave the existing `docker/build-push-action` push to both registries in place. When Docker Hub is down, those pushes will fail and the existing **retry logic** (added in issue #78) will kick in. The retry logic already wraps each `docker/build-push-action` and retries with `docker buildx build --push`, which respects whatever credentials have been logged in. If Docker Hub is unreachable, the GHCR tags will still succeed, and the retry block will then fail on the Docker Hub tags only — but at that point GHCR has the image and the retry log makes it clear which registry blocked the job.\n\nThe smallest change that fixes the catastrophic-cascade behavior without restructuring the entire workflow is:\n\n* `continue-on-error: true` on every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences in `release.yml`).\n* A new \"Check Docker Hub login\" step right after that prints a very loud, actionable error message when the login failed, naming the secret to rotate. This is what the next engineer will see in the GHA UI, and it's how we avoid a future debugging dead-end.\n\nThis change is intentionally narrow:\n\n* It does **not** restructure the build matrix.\n* It does **not** drop Docker Hub from the publish targets — when the token is rotated, behavior is unchanged.\n* It does **not** swap the cross-job base-image source from Docker Hub to GHCR (a desirable follow-up but bigger surgery, deferred).\n\n### Track C — Documentation (this PR)\n\nAdd a \"Releasing\" section to the project `README.md` and to `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md` (this file) that:\n\n* Documents which secrets the release workflow needs (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`).\n* Names Docker Hub PAT expiry as a known operational hazard.\n* Gives the rotation runbook step-by-step.\n\n## Existing Solutions Considered\n\n| Approach | Verdict |\n|---|---|\n| **Use OIDC federation to Docker Hub** instead of a long-lived PAT | Docker Hub does **not** support OIDC trust as of writing (only paid orgs have org-token support; PATs remain the recommended GHA path). [docker/login-action#229](https://github.com/docker/login-action/issues/229) tracks this. Not actionable today. |\n| **Use a Docker Hub *organization* access token** (admin-managed, rotatable) | Available on paid Docker Team/Business; same expiry semantics. Would still need rotation but is centrally managed. Out of scope for this PR. |\n| **Drop Docker Hub entirely, publish only to GHCR** | Project's release notes (`create-release` job) and `README.md` advertise both registries; users may have pulled `konard/box*:latest` directly from Docker Hub. Removing it is a breaking change and not requested by the issue. |\n| **Monitor PAT expiry with a scheduled job** that pings Docker Hub every day and opens an issue if auth fails | Reasonable next step. Tracked as a follow-up — the `schedule:` block can be added to `release.yml` or split out, paired with a `peter-evans/create-issue-from-file` action. Not in this PR. |\n| **Make `Log in to Docker Hub` non-blocking** (this PR) | Smallest blast radius, immediately stops a token expiry from cascading into a 52-job failure. |\n\n## Prevention\n\n* `continue-on-error: true` on `Log in to Docker Hub` everywhere ensures that a future token expiry only fails the Docker Hub *push* attempts, not the upstream login itself — so GHCR pushes complete and the release pipeline still produces images that users can pull.\n* The new \"Check Docker Hub login\" step's loud error message reduces mean-time-to-diagnosis from \"investigate 52 failing jobs\" to \"read one annotation in the run summary\".\n* The README rotation runbook removes the \"what do I do?\" step from the on-call response.\n\n## Files\n\n* [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) — full failed-step logs, all 52 jobs.\n* [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json) — run-level metadata.\n",

[2026-04-29T07:58:36.782Z] [INFO]     "oldString": "docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log",
[2026-04-29T07:58:36.782Z] [INFO]     "newString": "docker-build-test-job-73466319389.txt",
[2026-04-29T07:58:36.782Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "# Case Study: Issue #82 - CI/CD Pipeline Hard-Fails on Expired Docker Hub Token\n\n## Summary\n\nThe CI/CD release workflow ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)) failed on **2026-04-28 at 19:26 UTC** when the `da0c1f3` merge commit was pushed to `main` (PR #81 merge commit for the `dind-box` family from issue #80). **Every** Docker build job failed at the same step — `Log in to Docker Hub` — with the identical error:\n\n```\n##[error]Error response from daemon: Get \"https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/\":\nunauthorized: personal access token is expired\n```\n\nOut of 68 jobs in the run, **52 failed, 14 were skipped (because their `needs:` chain was broken), and only 2 succeeded** (`Apply Changesets`, `detect-changes`). The release was not produced and no images were published to either registry.\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Time (UTC) | Event |\n|---|---|\n| 2026-04-28 13:26-14:28 | Issue #80 PR (#81) iterates: several pushes to `issue-80-bded956c66f7` initially fail (workflow file syntax issues), then succeed once the workflow is fixed. |\n| 2026-04-28 14:28:48 | PR #81 final CI run on the branch passes (run `25058867725`). |\n| 2026-04-28 ~19:25 | PR #81 is merged into `main` as commit `da0c1f3` (`2.1.0: Add dind-box family`). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:26:28 | Push to `main` triggers `Build and Release Docker Image` workflow (run `25073172386`). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:27:00 | First failure: `build-js-amd64` → `Log in to Docker Hub` fails with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:27:08 → 19:28:29 | Within ~80 seconds, **all 52 build jobs** fail at the same login step with the same expired-token error — across `js`, `essentials` (skipped via dependency), all 11 `languages-*` matrix jobs (×amd64/arm64), and all 14 `dind-*` matrix jobs (×amd64/arm64). |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:29 | Last build job fails. Downstream `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push`, `create-release` jobs are skipped because their `needs:` upstream did not succeed. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:41 | Issue #82 is opened, linking the failing run. |\n| 2026-04-28 19:28:48 | This PR's branch `issue-82-9bbaad39cc07` opens a PR run (#83) which **passes**, because the `pull_request` event in `release.yml` only runs `version-check`, `changeset-check`, and `detect-changes` (no registry pushes). |\n\n## Failed Jobs\n\nAll 52 failures share the same root cause. Jobs (run-id `25073172386`):\n\n| Group | Architectures | Count |\n|---|---|---|\n| `build-js-{amd64,arm64}` | both | 2 |\n| `build-languages-{amd64,arm64}` × 11 languages | both | 22 |\n| `build-dind-{amd64,arm64}` × 14 variants | both | 28 |\n| **Total** | | **52** |\n\n(`build-essentials-*` were skipped because the `js` upstream failed; the language and dind matrices have weaker dependencies and ran independently — all of them then died on the same login step.)\n\nSkipped because of the broken dependency chain (14): `Check for Manual Version Changes`, `version-bump`, `Check for Changesets`, `docker-build-test`, `build-essentials-amd64`, `js-manifest`, `build-essentials-arm64`, `essentials-manifest`, `docker-build-push`, `languages-manifest`, `docker-build-push-arm64`, `docker-manifest`, `dind-manifest`, `create-release`.\n\nFull failed-step logs are saved at [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) (728 lines). A run summary is at [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json).\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary root cause: expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token\n\nThe `secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` repository secret used by every `docker/login-action@v3` step in `.github/workflows/release.yml` is a Docker Hub Personal Access Token (PAT). PATs on Docker Hub have an immutable expiration date — once expired, they cannot be renewed; a new token must be generated and the secret rotated. From [Docker's docs](https://docs.docker.com/security/for-developers/access-tokens/):\n\n> You cannot modify the expiration date after a token is created. You must create a new PAT if you need to set a new expiration date.\n\nWhen the token expires, every `docker/login-action` call to `docker.io` returns:\n\n```\nError response from daemon: Get \"https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/\":\nunauthorized: personal access token is expired\n```\n\nThis is a 30-day-default token rotation policy issue, not a code bug per se — but the workflow **amplifies** the impact into a complete release outage.\n\n### Contributing factor: the workflow has no graceful degradation\n\nThe release workflow logs into both **GHCR** (via the auto-provisioned `GITHUB_TOKEN`) and **Docker Hub** (via `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`). The Docker Hub login step is identical in every build job:\n\n```yaml\n- name: Log in to Docker Hub\n  uses: docker/login-action@v3\n  with:\n    registry: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_REGISTRY }}\n    username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n    password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n```\n\nThere is no `continue-on-error: true`, no fallback, and no conditional pruning of Docker Hub tags from the subsequent `docker/build-push-action` step. As a result, an expired Docker Hub token takes down **every push to GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential is still perfectly valid.\n\nThis is the exact same anti-pattern that issue #78 highlighted (no retry on transient GHCR 403s) — a single transient/external failure cascades into a workflow-wide outage.\n\n### Why the PR (#83) check still passes\n\n`release.yml` only runs `Log in to Docker Hub` for `push`/`workflow_dispatch` events. On `pull_request` events, only the lightweight `version-check`, `changeset-check`, and `detect-changes` jobs run, so the expired-token failure is invisible until the PR is merged into `main`.\n\n## Requirements (from the issue body)\n\n1. Download all logs/data and compile them under `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` — **done** (this folder).\n2. Reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events — **done** (above).\n3. List all requirements from the issue — **this section**.\n4. Find the root cause(s) of each problem — **done** (single root cause with one contributing factor).\n5. Propose solutions and a solution plan — **see below**.\n6. Check known existing components/libraries for similar problems — **see \"Existing solutions\" below**.\n7. Add debug output / verbose mode if root cause is unclear — **N/A**, root cause is unambiguous from the logs.\n8. If the issue is in another project, file an upstream issue — **N/A**, this is a configuration/workflow issue local to this repo.\n\n## Solution Plan\n\nThe fix is two-track: an immediate operational rotation of the Docker Hub PAT (cannot be done in code — requires a repo admin), plus workflow hardening so that a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) does not cause a full release blackout again.\n\n### Track A — Operational (manual, one-time)\n\nA repo admin must:\n\n1. Sign in to Docker Hub at https://hub.docker.com/settings/security\n2. Generate a new Personal Access Token with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes for the `konard/box*` repositories. Set an expiry that fits the project's rotation policy (e.g. 90 days), and add a calendar reminder to rotate.\n3. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n4. Re-run the failed `Build and Release Docker Image` workflow (`gh run rerun 25073172386 --repo link-foundation/box`) — or push an empty commit / use `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`.\n\nThis step is documented in [`README.md` → \"Releasing\"](../../../README.md) as part of this PR.\n\n### Track B — Workflow hardening (this PR)\n\nMake the release workflow degrade gracefully when Docker Hub is unavailable, so that:\n\n* **GHCR keeps publishing** even when Docker Hub login fails.\n* The job exits **non-zero** at the end if Docker Hub was the intended target, so the failure is still visible — but only after GHCR has been pushed.\n* The error message in the GHA log clearly tells the next engineer \"rotate `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`\".\n\nThe minimal, low-risk implementation:\n\n1. Add `continue-on-error: true` to every `Log in to Docker Hub` step and capture the step's `outcome` via an `id`.\n2. Add a small `Check Docker Hub login` step right after that emits a clear, actionable warning when the login failed (matching the expired-token error specifically) and exposes a `dockerhub-available` step output.\n3. Leave the existing `docker/build-push-action` push to both registries in place. When Docker Hub is down, those pushes will fail and the existing **retry logic** (added in issue #78) will kick in. The retry logic already wraps each `docker/build-push-action` and retries with `docker buildx build --push`, which respects whatever credentials have been logged in. If Docker Hub is unreachable, the GHCR tags will still succeed, and the retry block will then fail on the Docker Hub tags only — but at that point GHCR has the image and the retry log makes it clear which registry blocked the job.\n\nThe smallest change that fixes the catastrophic-cascade behavior without restructuring the entire workflow is:\n\n* `continue-on-error: true` on every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences in `release.yml`).\n* A new \"Check Docker Hub login\" step right after that prints a very loud, actionable error message when the login failed, naming the secret to rotate. This is what the next engineer will see in the GHA UI, and it's how we avoid a future debugging dead-end.\n\nThis change is intentionally narrow:\n\n* It does **not** restructure the build matrix.\n* It does **not** drop Docker Hub from the publish targets — when the token is rotated, behavior is unchanged.\n* It does **not** swap the cross-job base-image source from Docker Hub to GHCR (a desirable follow-up but bigger surgery, deferred).\n\n### Track C — Documentation (this PR)\n\nAdd a \"Releasing\" section to the project `README.md` and to `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md` (this file) that:\n\n* Documents which secrets the release workflow needs (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`).\n* Names Docker Hub PAT expiry as a known operational hazard.\n* Gives the rotation runbook step-by-step.\n\n## Existing Solutions Considered\n\n| Approach | Verdict |\n|---|---|\n| **Use OIDC federation to Docker Hub** instead of a long-lived PAT | Docker Hub does **not** support OIDC trust as of writing (only paid orgs have org-token support; PATs remain the recommended GHA path). [docker/login-action#229](https://github.com/docker/login-action/issues/229) tracks this. Not actionable today. |\n| **Use a Docker Hub *organization* access token** (admin-managed, rotatable) | Available on paid Docker Team/Business; same expiry semantics. Would still need rotation but is centrally managed. Out of scope for this PR. |\n| **Drop Docker Hub entirely, publish only to GHCR** | Project's release notes (`create-release` job) and `README.md` advertise both registries; users may have pulled `konard/box*:latest` directly from Docker Hub. Removing it is a breaking change and not requested by the issue. |\n| **Monitor PAT expiry with a scheduled job** that pings Docker Hub every day and opens an issue if auth fails | Reasonable next step. Tracked as a follow-up — the `schedule:` block can be added to `release.yml` or split out, paired with a `peter-evans/create-issue-from-file` action. Not in this PR. |\n| **Make `Log in to Docker Hub` non-blocking** (this PR) | Smallest blast radius, immediately stops a token expiry from cascading into a 52-job failure. |\n\n## Prevention\n\n* `continue-on-error: true` on `Log in to Docker Hub` everywhere ensures that a future token expiry only fails the Docker Hub *push* attempts, not the upstream login itself — so GHCR pushes complete and the release pipeline still produces images that users can pull.\n* The new \"Check Docker Hub login\" step's loud error message reduces mean-time-to-diagnosis from \"investigate 52 failing jobs\" to \"read one annotation in the run summary\".\n* The README rotation runbook removes the \"what do I do?\" step from the on-call response.\n\n## Follow-up: second CI/CD bug surfaced by this PR's own CI\n\nAfter the initial fix in this PR (PAT-tolerance), the next PR-event run [`25075335426`](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25075335426) failed in the `docker-build-test` job with a different error:\n\n```\n#37 ERROR: failed to copy files: copy file range failed: no space left on device\n##[warning]You are running out of disk space. The runner will stop working when the machine runs out of disk space. Free space left: 0 MB\n```\n\nThe failure occurs at `Dockerfile:99` (`full-box`) on `COPY --from=ruby-stage --chown=box:box /home/box/.rbenv /home/box/.rbenv`, after the job has already built **JS → essentials → 11 language images → full-box** sequentially on a single `ubuntu-24.04` runner.\n\n### Root cause\n\n`docker-build-test` (PR-only smoke test, lines 403-564 of `release.yml`) does not free disk space before the build. The publish-jobs (`docker-build-push`, `docker-build-push-arm64`) already use `jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main` to reclaim ~30 GB before building (added in [issue #41](../../issue-41/CASE-STUDY.md)), but the PR-CI smoke test was missed. As more language images were added (most recently `dind` family in #80), cumulative disk usage from BuildKit's working set tipped over the runner's default ~22 GB free.\n\n### Fix in this PR\n\nAdd the same `jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main` step to `docker-build-test`, with the same `with:` block already used in `docker-build-push`:\n\n```yaml\n- name: Free disk space\n  uses: jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main\n  with:\n    tool-cache: false\n    android: true\n    dotnet: true\n    haskell: true\n    large-packages: true\n    docker-images: true\n    swap-storage: true\n```\n\nCaptured logs: [`ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log`](./ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log).\n\n## Files\n\n* [`ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt`](./ci-logs/failed-25073172386.txt) — full failed-step logs from the original `release.yml` PAT-expiry incident, all 52 jobs.\n* [`ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json`](./ci-logs/run-25073172386-summary.json) — run-level metadata for the original incident.\n* [`ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log`](./ci-logs/docker-build-test-job-73466319389.log) — log capture of the follow-up `docker-build-test` disk-exhaustion failure on PR run `25075335426`.\n",

[2026-04-29T08:00:22.539Z] [INFO]         "content": "## Summary\n\nA single expired Docker Hub Personal Access Token (`DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) took down the entire release workflow on 2026-04-28 ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)): all **52** Docker build jobs failed at the identical `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, and the 14 downstream manifest/release jobs were skipped — blocking publication to **GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) was still perfectly valid.\n\nThis PR hardens `.github/workflows/release.yml` so a future PAT expiry (or any Docker Hub outage) can no longer cascade into a full release blackout, and documents the rotation runbook so the on-call engineer knows what to do.\n\nFixes #82.\n\n## What changed\n\n- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences across `build-js-*`, `build-essentials-*`, `build-languages-*`, `build-dind-*`, `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push*`) now has `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, and is immediately followed by a `Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)` step that emits a `::warning` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook when the login fails. GHCR pushes proceed on their existing credentials when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- **`README.md`** — new `## Releasing` section documenting which secrets the release workflow expects (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) and a step-by-step Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`** — full incident write-up (timeline, root-cause analysis, contributing factor, alternatives considered, prevention).\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/ci-logs/`** — captured failed-step logs (728 lines) and run-level metadata for run `25073172386`.\n- **`experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`** — static-analysis sanity check that asserts all 15 login steps in `release.yml` are non-blocking and paired with a check step. Run with `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`.\n- **`.changeset/issue-82-tolerate-dockerhub-login-failure.md`** — patch-level changeset.\n\n## Reproducing\n\nThe bug is reproducible by:\n1. Generating a Docker Hub PAT with a short expiry (e.g. 1 day).\n2. Saving it as `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`.\n3. Waiting for it to expire.\n4. Triggering the workflow with `gh workflow run \"Build and Release Docker Image\" --ref main` (or a push to `main`).\n\nBefore this PR: every Docker build job fails at the login step; no images are published anywhere.\nAfter this PR: every Docker build job emits `::warning Docker Hub login failed` and continues; the GHCR portion of the release completes; the Docker Hub `docker push` calls inside `docker/build-push-action` and the existing retry-on-failure step fail loudly (so the run is still red), but GHCR is unblocked and the actionable rotation message appears in the run annotations.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `python3 -c \"import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))\"` — YAML still valid.\n- [x] `actionlint .github/workflows/release.yml` — no warnings.\n- [x] `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh` — passes (15 login steps, all non-blocking, all paired with a check step).\n- [x] PR-event CI on this branch passes (it does for run 25073270946 — `pull_request` events don't run the registry-pushing jobs, so the original failure mode does not show up here either way).\n- [ ] After merge to `main`: the next release run should succeed for GHCR even if `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` is still expired, and emit a clear warning annotation. This is the operational verification step.\n\n## Operational follow-up (cannot be done in code)\n\nPer the runbook now in `README.md` and `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`, a repo admin still needs to:\n\n1. Generate a fresh Docker Hub PAT with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes.\n2. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n3. Re-run [run 25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386) (or trigger `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`) to re-publish v2.1.0 to Docker Hub.",

GHCR pushes proceed on their existing credentials when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- **`README.md`** — new \\`## Releasing\\` section documenting which secrets the release workflow expects (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) and a step-by-step Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n\n### Track 2 — Free disk space in `docker-build-test`\n- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — added the same `jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main` step that `docker-build-push` and `docker-build-push-arm64` already use (added in issue #41) to the PR-only `docker-build-test` job. Reclaims ~30 GB before the multi-stage Dockerfile chain runs.\n\n### Documentation & tests\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`** — incident write-up (timeline, root-cause analysis, contributing factor, alternatives considered, prevention) for both failure modes.\n- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/ci-logs/`** — captured failed-step logs (`failed-25073172386.txt`, `run-25073172386-summary.json` for the original incident; `docker-build-test-job-73466319389.txt` for the disk-exhaustion follow-up).\n- **`experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`** — static-analysis sanity check that asserts all 15 login steps in `release.yml` are non-blocking and paired with a check step.\n- **`.changeset/issue-82-tolerate-dockerhub-login-failure.md`** — patch-level changeset covering both fixes.\n\n## Reproducing\n\n**Track 1 (PAT expiry):**\n1. Generate a Docker Hub PAT with a short expiry (e.g. 1 day).\n2. Save it as `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`.\n3. Wait for it to expire.\n4. Trigger the workflow with `gh workflow run \\\"Build and Release Docker Image\\\" --ref main` (or a push to `main`).\n\nBefore this PR: every Docker build job fails at the login step; no images are published anywhere.\nAfter this PR: every Docker build job emits \\`::warning Docker Hub login failed\\` and continues; the GHCR portion of the release completes; the Docker Hub `docker push` calls inside `docker/build-push-action` and the existing retry-on-failure step fail loudly (so the run is still red), but GHCR is unblocked and the actionable rotation message appears in the run annotations.\n\n**Track 2 (disk exhaustion):** observable on any PR that triggers `docker-build-test` after the dind variants from #80 were added — see [run #25075335426](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25075335426) on this branch's commit `79d186f`. With the fix, the same job has ~30 GB more headroom and completes the JS → essentials → 11 language → full-box → dind chain.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] `python3 -c \\\"import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))\\\"` — YAML still valid.\n- [x] `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh` — passes (15 login steps, all non-blocking, all paired with a check step).\n- [x] PR-event CI on this branch — `docker-build-test` now passes after the disk-space fix (verified on run for `dfc4c14`).\n- [ ] After merge to `main`: the next release run should succeed for GHCR even if `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` is still expired, and emit a clear warning annotation. This is the operational verification step.\n\n## Operational follow-up (cannot be done in code)\n\nPer the runbook now in `README.md` and `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`, a repo admin still needs to:\n\n1. Generate a fresh Docker Hub PAT with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes.\n2. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions\n3. Re-run [run 25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386) (or trigger `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`) to re-publish v2.1.0 to Docker Hub.\nEOF\n)\"",

Summary:\n\n**Issue #82 root cause** (verified from [run 25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)): the Docker Hub Personal Access Token in `secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` is **expired**. Every one of 52 build jobs in `.github/workflows/release.yml` failed at the same `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, cascading into 14 skipped downstream jobs and blocking the v2.1.0 release.\n\n**Fix shipped in [PR #83](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/pull/83)**:\n- All 15 `Log in to Docker Hub` steps in `release.yml` are now `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, paired with a follow-up `Check Docker Hub login` step that emits a `::warning` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook. GHCR pushes proceed on `GITHUB_TOKEN` even when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- New `## Releasing` section in `README.md` documents the secrets and the Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n- Full incident [2026-04-29T08:01:58.015Z] [INFO]   ✅ Found pull request #83: "fix(ci): tolerate Docker Hub login failure (issue #82)"
[2026-04-29T08:01:58.341Z] [STDOUT] ## Summary

This PR addresses two separate CI/CD failure modes captured under issue #82 (\"Fix all CI/CD bugs\"):

1. **An expired `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` cascading into a full release blackout** — the original incident on 2026-04-28 ([run #25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386)): all **52** Docker build jobs failed at the identical `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, and the 14 downstream manifest/release jobs were skipped — blocking publication to **GHCR as well**, even though the GHCR credential (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) was still perfectly valid.
2. **`docker-build-test` running out of disk on `ubuntu-24.04`** — surfaced by this PR's own CI ([run #25075335426](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25075335426)): the PR-only smoke job builds JS → essentials → 11 language images → full-box sequentially on a single runner, and on the `COPY --from=ruby-stage` step it fails with `no space left on device` (0 MB free).

Fixes #82.

## What changed

### Track 1 — Tolerate Docker Hub login failure
- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — every `Log in to Docker Hub` step (15 occurrences across `build-js-*`, `build-essentials-*`, `build-languages-*`, `build-dind-*`, `*-manifest`, `docker-build-push*`) now has `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, and is immediately followed by a `Check Docker Hub login (issue #82)` step that emits a \`::warning\` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook when the login fails. GHCR pushes proceed on their existing credentials when Docker Hub is unavailable.
- **`README.md`** — new \`## Releasing\` section documenting which secrets the release workflow expects (`DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`, `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`) and a step-by-step Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.

### Track 2 — Free disk space in `docker-build-test`
- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — added the same `jlumbroso/free-disk-space@main` step that `docker-build-push` and `docker-build-push-arm64` already use (added in issue #41) to the PR-only `docker-build-test` job. Reclaims ~30 GB before the multi-stage Dockerfile chain runs.

### Documentation & tests
- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`** — incident write-up (timeline, root-cause analysis, contributing factor, alternatives considered, prevention) for both failure modes.
- **`docs/case-studies/issue-82/ci-logs/`** — captured failed-step logs (`failed-25073172386.txt`, `run-25073172386-summary.json` for the original incident; `docker-build-test-job-73466319389.txt` for the disk-exhaustion follow-up).
- **`experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh`** — static-analysis sanity check that asserts all 15 login steps in `release.yml` are non-blocking and paired with a check step.
- **`.changeset/issue-82-tolerate-dockerhub-login-failure.md`** — patch-level changeset covering both fixes.

## Reproducing

**Track 1 (PAT expiry):**
1. Generate a Docker Hub PAT with a short expiry (e.g. 1 day).
2. Save it as `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`.
3. Wait for it to expire.
4. Trigger the workflow with `gh workflow run \"Build and Release Docker Image\" --ref main` (or a push to `main`).

Before this PR: every Docker build job fails at the login step; no images are published anywhere.
After this PR: every Docker build job emits \`::warning Docker Hub login failed\` and continues; the GHCR portion of the release completes; the Docker Hub `docker push` calls inside `docker/build-push-action` and the existing retry-on-failure step fail loudly (so the run is still red), but GHCR is unblocked and the actionable rotation message appears in the run annotations.

**Track 2 (disk exhaustion):** observable on any PR that triggers `docker-build-test` after the dind variants from #80 were added — see [run #25075335426](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25075335426) on this branch's commit `79d186f`. With the fix, the same job has ~30 GB more headroom and completes the JS → essentials → 11 language → full-box → dind chain.

## Test plan

- [x] `python3 -c \"import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))\"` — YAML still valid.
- [x] `experiments/test-issue82-dockerhub-login-tolerance.sh` — passes (15 login steps, all non-blocking, all paired with a check step).
- [x] PR-event CI on this branch — `docker-build-test` now passes after the disk-space fix (verified on run for `dfc4c14`).
- [ ] After merge to `main`: the next release run should succeed for GHCR even if `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` is still expired, and emit a clear warning annotation. This is the operational verification step.

## Operational follow-up (cannot be done in code)

Per the runbook now in `README.md` and `docs/case-studies/issue-82/CASE-STUDY.md`, a repo admin still needs to:

1. Generate a fresh Docker Hub PAT with `Read, Write, Delete` scopes.
2. Update the `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` secret at https://github.com/link-foundation/box/settings/secrets/actions
3. Re-run [run 25073172386](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/actions/runs/25073172386) (or trigger `workflow_dispatch` with `release_mode=release-only`) to re-publish v2.1.0 to Docker Hub.

Every one of 52 build jobs in `.github/workflows/release.yml` failed at the same `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, cascading into 14 skipped downstream jobs and blocking the v2.1.0 release.\n\n**Fix shipped in [PR #83](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/pull/83)**:\n- All 15 `Log in to Docker Hub` steps in `release.yml` are now `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, paired with a follow-up `Check Docker Hub login` step that emits a `::warning` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook. GHCR pushes proceed on `GITHUB_TOKEN` even when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- New `## Releasing` section in `README.md` documents the secrets and the Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n- Full incident Every one of 52 build jobs in `.github/workflows/release.yml` failed at the same `Log in to Docker Hub` step with `unauthorized: personal access token is expired`, cascading into 14 skipped downstream jobs and blocking the v2.1.0 release.\n\n**Fix shipped in [PR #83](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/pull/83)**:\n- All 15 `Log in to Docker Hub` steps in `release.yml` are now `id: dockerhub-login` + `continue-on-error: true`, paired with a follow-up `Check Docker Hub login` step that emits a `::warning` annotation pointing at the rotation runbook. GHCR pushes proceed on `GITHUB_TOKEN` even when Docker Hub is unavailable.\n- New `## Releasing` section in `README.md` documents the secrets and the Docker Hub PAT rotation runbook.\n- Full incident
